Title: Senior Data Analyst (m/f/x)
Description:
Atolls is the world’s largest community-driven shopping platform, active in 20+ markets.

We help millions make smarter spending decisions across discovery, evaluation, and (re-)purchase by connecting people with the right brands and retailers.

Our 1,000+ team across 10 countries builds products used every day at global scale, where you’ll have real ownership and see your impact. Want to shape the destinations people rely on to shop with confidence? Keep reading.




About This Role:

We are looking for a Senior Data Analyst (Community & Product Forensics) to join our Community Data Team team in Berlin and surrounding area. At Atolls, your role will be instrumental in helping us lead our community intelligence and data integrity efforts. You will be our "Data Detective," responsible for performing deep-dive analytics on high-volume event data, ensuring its accuracy, and uncovering insights to drive community growth. As a key part of our team, you'll be directly involved in building and enhancing the digital destinations our users rely on throughout their shopping journeys.

Your contributions will help shape how millions of consumers interact with our platforms, guiding them to make smart, fair, and rewarding choices.




Our Benefits:

At Atolls, we believe in nurturing both your professional and personal growth. Here’s what you can expect:

A culture that values personal and professional development, with internal mobility opportunities.
A supportive and open-minded team that embraces diverse perspectives and innovative ideas.
32 days of paid vacation plus your birthday off, giving you the time you need to recharge.
A flexible hybrid working scheme to balance work and life.
Access to a learning budget and internal training to help you grow in your role.
Mental health coaching to support your well-being.
Regular global and local get-togethers to celebrate successes and build connections.
The possibility of taking a sabbatical after three years with the company.
A cloud-based company setup, providing flexibility and collaboration opportunities no matter where you are.
These are global benefits that apply to all employees, with additional local perks based on your location.




Responsibilities:

In this role, you will:

Technical Investigation & Forensics: Identify and resolve data discrepancies. If a metric looks "off," you'll use your knowledge of web/app technologies (inspecting network requests, tracking pixels, or API responses) to find the root cause.
Deep-Dive Community Analytics: Analyze complex member behaviors—retention cohorts, engagement velocity, and viral loops—to drive community growth.
Data Science & Modeling: Move beyond descriptive stats by building and deploying models (e.g., Churn Prediction, Sentiment Analysis, or Member Segmentation) using Python.
High-Performance SQL: Master our ClickHouse environment, writing optimized queries that leverage columnar storage to process billions of rows of interaction data.
Data Hygiene: Audit "dirty" data and build robust logic to handle bot activity, duplicate identities, and tracking gaps.




Your Profile:

We're looking for someone with experience in writing complex SQL, investigating web/app event data, and building predictive models in Python, who is dedicated to creating exceptional user experiences and driving innovation.

Expert SQL: Advanced mastery of SQL (CTEs, Window Functions, Optimization) specifically for columnar databases (ClickHouse experience is a massive plus).
Web/App Technical Literacy: Basic understanding of how web/app tracking works. You should be comfortable using tools to inspect network payloads and debug event triggers.
Analytical Intuition: A "sixth sense" for spotting data anomalies and the tenacity to investigate them until they are solved.




Nice-to-Haves:

Workflow Automation: Basic knowledge of Airflow for scheduling analytical scripts.
Data Science Toolkit: Proficiency in Python (Pandas, Scikit-learn) for statistical modeling and complex data manipulation.
Community Domain Knowledge: Experience analyzing developer communities, forums, or social platforms.




Our hiring process:

TA Call: Meet one of our Talent Experts and get to know Atolls better.
Technical Round: Focus on the technical aspects of the role (Through a Live Case), and meet your potential manager.
Final Round: Meet other Atollians 🏝️ It varies from 1 to 3 interviews
Some processes might slightly change according to needs




Ready to apply?

Be part of a destination where your work helps millions of people make better decisions every day. One focused application is all we need. If you truly fit more than one role, you’re welcome to apply to up to three. This helps us match you with the right opportunity faster.




We review every application with equal care and will reach out if your profile aligns. Apply now with your CV in English.

Portfolios, writing samples, or certifications may be requested based on the role.




At Atolls, we want to ensure that all employees can thrive in an inclusive environment. Our employment opportunities are open to every gender, race, religion, age, sexual orientation, ability, place of origin, or socioeconomic status. We remain committed to a culture of diversity, equity and belonging, where all employees are welcomed, respected, connected, and engaged.

Title: Market Data Engineer
Description:
Location: Potsdam office, hybrid work model, applicants from Berlin and Brandenburg welcome

Language: Fluent in English




Company Description

Index Bakery is a financial technology company building modern infrastructure for the creation, calculation, and publication of financial indices. Our platform combines index engineering, market data processing, and operational tooling to help institutional clients launch and maintain index products efficiently and reliably.




We work at the intersection of finance, data, and software engineering. Our systems are designed to handle complex market data, support robust index calculations, and provide the transparency, auditability, and reliability required in a production financial environment.




As an early-stage company, we are building both the technology and the organization from the ground up. This creates a hands-on environment where engineers can take real ownership, contribute to core product decisions, and help shape how financial index infrastructure should work in the future.




Role Overview

We’re looking for a Market Data Engineer who thrives on transforming complex market data into clean, validated, and production-ready systems. You’ll be responsible for integrating data from multiple vendors, building robust data pipelines, and ensuring that every number can be trusted.




This role sits at the intersection of software engineering, financial market data, and data quality. You do not need to be a financial markets expert from day one, but you should be curious, structured, and eager to learn how market data, instruments, and index calculation processes work in production.




Why Join Us

At Index Bakery, you will work on systems that directly power the creation, calculation, and publication of financial indices used by institutional clients. We are a small and highly technical team where engineers have real ownership, direct impact, and the opportunity to shape both the platform and the company from an early stage.




As the company grows, you will have the opportunity to contribute not only to our systems, but also to the evolution of our data platform, products, and engineering practices.




Responsibilities

Integrate market data from external vendors, exchanges, and internal sources.
Build, maintain, and improve data ingestion pipelines for prices, reference data, FX rates, corporate actions, calendars, and other market data.
Normalize heterogeneous data formats into clean, consistent, and well-documented internal data models.
Implement validation checks, reconciliation logic, and data quality controls to detect missing, stale, inconsistent, or suspicious data.
Work closely with Index Engineering and Index Operations to ensure data is fit for backtesting, daily index calculation, reporting, and publication.
Investigate data issues in production and help identify root causes across vendors, pipelines, storage, and downstream systems.
Design pragmatic solutions for historical data management, versioning, corrections, and auditability of market data.
Contribute to internal tooling, APIs, monitoring, documentation, and operational workflows around market data.
Help shape the Market Data Platform as the company grows, balancing speed, reliability, and simplicity.




Requirements

3+ years of professional experience in software engineering, data engineering, or a closely related role.
Strong Python skills and the ability to write clean, maintainable, production-oriented code.
Solid SQL skills and practical experience working with relational databases.
Experience building or maintaining data pipelines, ETL/ELT processes, APIs, or backend services.
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure, ideally GCP, AWS, or Azure.
Understanding of data quality concepts such as validation, reconciliation, completeness checks, and auditability.
Ability to reason carefully about edge cases, data inconsistencies, and operational failure modes.
Comfortable working in a small team where ownership, communication, and pragmatic decision-making matter.
Fluent English communication skills.




Nice to Have

Experience with financial market data, index data, asset management, trading, exchanges, or data vendors.
Familiarity with instruments such as equities, bonds, ETFs, commodities, FX, derivatives, or crypto assets.
Experience with tools such as Docker, FastAPI, Pydantic, Airflow, Dagster, dbt, BigQuery, Cloud SQL, or similar technologies.
Experience with data historization, slowly changing dimensions, event-based data, or audit trails.
Experience operating production systems, including monitoring, alerting, incident analysis, and debugging.
Interest in financial markets, data modelling, and building systems where correctness matters.




What We Value

We care deeply about the mindset you bring to the team. We are looking for someone who is humble, curious, and eager to improve. Someone who enjoys learning new domains, asks thoughtful questions, and is comfortable saying, “I don’t know yet, but I’ll find out.”




We believe great engineers never stop learning. We value intellectual honesty, ownership, and collaboration. We are less interested in people who always have an answer, and more interested in people who are willing to challenge assumptions, learn from others, and continuously grow.




You should enjoy working through messy real-world data problems with patience and precision. We value people who take ownership without ego, communicate clearly, and want to build reliable systems together with a small, ambitious team.




What You'll Work On

At Index Bakery, market data is a core part of our infrastructure. Your work will directly support the calculation, validation, and publication of financial indices. You’ll help build the data foundation that enables index backtests, production calculations, client reports, operational checks, and future data products.




This is a hands-on engineering role with real ownership from the beginning.

Title: Analytics Engineer (m/w/d)
Description:
Wir sind nicht der lokale Stromanbieter um die Ecke. Wir sind das globale EnerTech-Unternehmen, das den Energiemarkt revolutionieren wird. Wir haben kein geringeres Ziel, als die global benötigte Energie 100% nachhaltig zu gestalten. Global, agil und zukunftsorientiert verändern wir den Energiemarkt mit neuer Technologie zum Besseren.




Dabei sind unsere Ökostromtarife nur die Basis. Bereits seit 2022 installieren wir Wärmepumpen-Komplettlösungen mit eigenen Fachkräften. Außerdem arbeiten wir aktiv an der flächendeckenden Einführung von Smart Metern, Elektromobilitätslösungen und der eigenen Stromerzeugung aus Wind- und Sonnenenergie.




Das schaffen wir allerdings nicht alleine, sondern dazu brauchen wir dich! Werde Teil unseres Teams und fordere den Status Quo heraus. Mit Energie, die gut für den Planeten ist und gut für dein Karma. 💚  Finde jetzt deinen Traumjob und werde Teil der Energierevolution von Octopus Energy Germany.




Für unser Analytics-Team suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt engagierte Analytics Engineers (m/w/d) für zwei spannende Schwerpunkte: Gross Margin & Cost Modelling. 




Beide Rollen leisten einen zentralen Beitrag zur datengetriebenen Steuerung unseres Energiegeschäfts und verbinden moderne Datenarchitekturen mit geschäftskritischen Entscheidungen. Je nach Schwerpunkt arbeitest du entweder an der Transparenz und Optimierung unserer Bruttomarge oder an der Weiterentwicklung unserer Kosten- und Pricing-Logik. Dabei entwickelst du skalierbare Datenprodukte, modellierst komplexe Datenlandschaften und schaffst die Grundlage für fundierte Entscheidungen in einem dynamischen Marktumfeld.


Schwerpunkt: Gross Margin


In diesem Bereich bist du das technische Rückgrat unserer Margensteuerung. Du baust und verantwortest zentrale Datenpipelines, die Verbrauchs-, Kosten-, Umsatz- und Rechnungsdaten zusammenführen, um ein vollständiges Bild unserer Bruttomarge zu schaffen. Du liebst es, große und komplexe Datenmengen performant zu modellieren und darauf aufbauend skalierbare Data Products zu entwickeln, die unser Business messbar voranbringen.


Schwerpunkt: Cost Modelling


In diesem Bereich hebst du unsere Datenarchitektur rund um direkte Kosten wie Netzentgelte, Umlagen und Steuern auf das nächste Level. Du fungierst als Bindeglied zwischen komplexen regulatorischen Anforderungen, energiewirtschaftlichen Forecasts und unserer Pricing-Strategie. Dabei entwickelst du skalierbare Datenpipelines, behältst auch bei sich wandelnden regulatorischen Rahmenbedingungen den Überblick und gestaltest durch moderne Architekturansätze die Zukunftsfähigkeit unserer Systeme aktiv mit.




 




Deine Aufgaben:Datenarchitektur & Pipeline Engineering



Du konzipierst, entwickelst und betreibst skalierbare Datenpipelines und Datenmodelle, die geschäftskritische Prozesse im Energievertrieb unterstützen


Du integrierst und verknüpfst komplexe Datenquellen aus internen Systemen, Marktdaten sowie regulatorischen Quellen und stellst deren konsistente Verarbeitung sicher


Du verantwortest die Transformation, Modellierung und Aufbereitung großer Datenmengen, um fundierte Analysen und datengetriebene Entscheidungen zu ermöglichen




Data Engineering & Plattform-Exzellenz



Du optimierst Datenmodelle und Pipelines hinsichtlich Performance, Skalierbarkeit und Wartbarkeit


Mit modernen Tools wie dbt und Airflow stellst du eine hohe Datenqualität, Stabilität und Transparenz unserer Datenlandschaft sicher


Du treibst die Weiterentwicklung unserer Datenarchitektur voran und etablierst Best Practices im Bereich Analytics Engineering




Business-nahe Datenprodukte & Modellierung



Du entwickelst skalierbare Data Products und analytische Modelle, die zentrale Geschäftsentscheidungen in den Bereichen Pricing, Kostensteuerung und Profitabilität unterstützen


Du übersetzt komplexe fachliche Anforderungen - von energiewirtschaftlichen Forecasts bis hin zu regulatorischen Rahmenbedingungen - in robuste und performante Datenlösungen


Du stellst sicher, dass fachliche und regulatorische Änderungen zeitnah in unseren Datenmodellen und Prozessen berücksichtigt werden




Zusammenarbeit & Impact



Du arbeitest eng mit Stakeholdern aus Pricing, Controlling, Energy Markets und weiteren Fachbereichen zusammen, um datengetriebene Lösungen für komplexe Business-Fragestellungen zu entwickeln


Du fungierst als Sparringspartner für analytische und technische Fragestellungen und sorgst dafür, dass unsere Datenprodukte einen direkten Mehrwert für das Business schaffen


Du unterstützt die kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung unseres Analytics-Teams durch Wissensaustausch, technische Standards und moderne Engineering-Praktiken







Dein Background:


Du hast ein Studium in (Wirtschafts-)Informatik, Mathematik, Statistik, Wirtschaftswissenschaften oder einem ähnlichen quantitativen Fach erfolgreich abgeschlossen


Du bringst fundierte Berufserfahrung (Mid-Level bis Senior) als Analytics Engineer, Data Engineer oder in einer vergleichbaren datengetriebenen Rolle mit


Du bist ein Profi im Umgang mit SQL und Python. Idealerweise hast du bereits Erfahrung mit dbt und Apache Airflow in produktiven Umgebungen gesammelt


Du hast ein ausgeprägtes Verständnis für Datenarchitekturen und die Fähigkeit, dynamische fachliche/regulatorische Anforderungen in sauberen, wartbaren Code zu übersetzen


Du arbeitest analytisch, konzeptionell und findest pragmatische Lösungen für technische Herausforderungen


Du kommunizierst klar - auf Deutsch und Englisch - und kannst fachliche Komplexität für Stakeholder verständlich aufbereiten


Du hast eine ausgeprägte Ownership-Mentalität: Du baust Systeme nicht nur, du verantwortest ihren Betrieb und ihre kontinuierliche Anpassung an den Markt










Deine Benefits bei uns:




✅ Vergünstigungen & Vorsorge:

- Aktienoptionen der Octopus Energy Group Ltd.

- Vergünstigte Konditionen für unsere Strom- und Gastarife

- Monatlicher Zuschuss zum Nahverkehrsticket

- Monatlicher Zuschuss zum Mittagessen

- Vergünstigtes Fahrradleasing

- Betriebliche Altersvorsorge




✅ Familie

- Beratungsangebote und Unterstützung bei Kinderbetreuung über HeyNanny

- Monatlicher Zuschuss zur Kinderbetreuung

- Elterngeld-Bonus: Wir stocken dein Elterngeld für 3 Monate auf, damit du deine Elternzeit entspannter genießen kannst 




✅ Sport & Gesundheit

- Zuzahlungen zu Fitness- Angeboten über Wellpass

- Förderung deines mentalen Wohlbefindens mit unserem Partner Open Up




✅ Lernen & Weiterbildung

- Attraktive Weiterbildungsmöglichkeiten und Aufstiegsmöglichkeiten

- Kostenlose Sprachkurse über Busuu

- Gutschein zu deinem Geburtstag

HTML:
Wir sind nicht der lokale Stromanbieter um die Ecke. Wir sind das globale EnerTech-Unternehmen, das den Energiemarkt revolutionieren wird. Wir haben kein geringeres Ziel, als die global benötigte Energie 100% nachhaltig zu gestalten. Global, agil und zukunftsorientiert verändern wir den Energiemarkt mit neuer Technologie zum Besseren.


Dabei sind unsere Ökostromtarife nur die Basis. Bereits seit 2022 installieren wir Wärmepumpen-Komplettlösungen mit eigenen Fachkräften. Außerdem arbeiten wir aktiv an der flächendeckenden Einführung von Smart Metern, Elektromobilitätslösungen und der eigenen Stromerzeugung aus Wind- und Sonnenenergie.


Das schaffen wir allerdings nicht alleine, sondern dazu brauchen wir dich! Werde Teil unseres Teams und fordere den Status Quo heraus. Mit Energie, die gut für den Planeten ist und gut für dein Karma. 💚  Finde jetzt deinen Traumjob und werde Teil der Energierevolution von Octopus Energy Germany.


Für unser Analytics-Team suchen wir zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt engagierte Analytics Engineers (m/w/d) für zwei spannende Schwerpunkte: Gross Margin & Cost Modelling. 


Beide Rollen leisten einen zentralen Beitrag zur datengetriebenen Steuerung unseres Energiegeschäfts und verbinden moderne Datenarchitekturen mit geschäftskritischen Entscheidungen. Je nach Schwerpunkt arbeitest du entweder an der Transparenz und Optimierung unserer Bruttomarge oder an der Weiterentwicklung unserer Kosten- und Pricing-Logik. Dabei entwickelst du skalierbare Datenprodukte, modellierst komplexe Datenlandschaften und schaffst die Grundlage für fundierte Entscheidungen in einem dynamischen Marktumfeld.


Schwerpunkt: Gross Margin

In diesem Bereich bist du das technische Rückgrat unserer Margensteuerung. Du baust und verantwortest zentrale Datenpipelines, die Verbrauchs-, Kosten-, Umsatz- und Rechnungsdaten zusammenführen, um ein vollständiges Bild unserer Bruttomarge zu schaffen. Du liebst es, große und komplexe Datenmengen performant zu modellieren und darauf aufbauend skalierbare Data Products zu entwickeln, die unser Business messbar voranbringen.


Schwerpunkt: Cost Modelling

In diesem Bereich hebst du unsere Datenarchitektur rund um direkte Kosten wie Netzentgelte, Umlagen und Steuern auf das nächste Level. Du fungierst als Bindeglied zwischen komplexen regulatorischen Anforderungen, energiewirtschaftlichen Forecasts und unserer Pricing-Strategie. Dabei entwickelst du skalierbare Datenpipelines, behältst auch bei sich wandelnden regulatorischen Rahmenbedingungen den Überblick und gestaltest durch moderne Architekturansätze die Zukunftsfähigkeit unserer Systeme aktiv mit.

Title: Junior Tracking Specialist (all genders)
Description:
Your role

You will support the measurement of user behavior and E-Commerce data on our websites and possible future app(s). For this, you will help continuously evolve the client- and server-side Google Tag Manager setup, including the usage of accompanying features in Google Cloud, as well as improving the GA4 setup.

The position centers around supporting to set up the best possible tracking to provide high-quality data to be used in our marketing channels, for website optimization, and in BI reporting to facilitate further business growth.

You will be an important contributor to the company's website data setup for our 10+ country domains together with our Senior Tracking Specialist.


Continuously improve the quality and quantity of measured data in GTM and GA 4
Support implementing data quality monitoring measures and creating suitable reports with Looker Studio
Participate in shaping our measurement strategy to meet marketing and business needs
Take over parts of the maintenance of Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics
Educate stakeholders about the features and limitations of our tracking setup
Continuously monitor new developments in the digital analytics ecosphere
Collaborate closely with our marketing and data teams to solve business questions, create synergies, and drive business growth through data
Support marketing teams with requests


Your profile


At least 1 year of in-depth experience with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4; App tracking is a bonus
Demonstrated success in the development of tracking setups
Experience with setting up Server-Side GTM in a multi-domain setup
You are accustomed to the tracking needs of paid channels such as Google Ads, Meta Ads, and others
Basic knowledge in SQL, CSS, and JS is a plus
Strong focus on structured approaches, documentation, and organizing maintenance and monitoring of data quality
High interest in closely collaborating with Data and Marketing Teams
Talent to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
An interest in how AI will augment work in data measurement and digital analytics is a plus


What we offer


An appealing discount on all of our products – from essential oils to vitamins & nutrients
We offer 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 days off between Christmas and New Year's (pro rata), provided Sunday Natural is closed for company holidays during this time
The Urban Sports Club membership and either Swapfiets bike rental or a subsidy for the Deutschlandticket
We believe great things happen when we meet in person, which is why we embrace a hybrid model with one home office day per week – up to two depending on the area you are working in – and up to two weeks per year working from abroad
Receive various discounts of up to more than 50 percent on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia
You will be given real ownership and plenty of room for new initiatives, as well as the opportunity to contribute your expertise and implement your great ideas
Inviting office spaces with access to plenty of organic teas and superfoods from around the world
We like to celebrate each other! Regular tea & office parties, Christmas and summer feasts and various influencer events such as Women's Day


Apply with your CV and cover letter.

Apply for this job

About Us

Sunday Natural emerged from a long-standing passion and research in the fields of health, healing, and personal growth. The lack of natural, high-quality products on the market was the original motivation for founding Sunday Natural in 2013. We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 40 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

Title: Analytics Engineer (Power BI Specialist)
Description:
Company overview:

Blue Orange Digital is a boutique data & AI consultancy that delivers enterprise-grade results. We design and build modern data platforms, analytics, and ML/AI Agent solutions for mid‑market and enterprise clients across Private Equity, Financial Services, Healthcare, and Retail.

Our teams work with technologies like Databricks, Snowflake, dbt, and the broader Microsoft ecosystem to turn messy, real-world data into trustworthy, actionable insight.

We’re a builder‑led, client‑first culture that prizes ownership, clear communication, and shipping high‑impact work.

Note: Please submit your resume in English, as all application materials must be in English for review and consideration.

Position Overview

Blue Orange Digital is seeking a Senior Analytics Engineer and Power BI Specialist to own the analytics and visualization layer of a 26‑week enterprise data warehouse implementation in the global commodities trading and supply chain industry. You will join full‑time from Week 6 through Week 22, designing and building Fabric Semantic Models and production Power BI reports that translate Gold‑layer data products into operational dashboards for trading, risk, and finance stakeholders — including Trader P&L, Global Position, Risk Metrics (VaR), and Financial Consolidation.

This role bridges the engineering team and business users, requiring deep Power BI expertise and the communication skills to work directly with trading and finance professionals.

Responsibilities


Design and build Microsoft Fabric Semantic Models (Power BI datasets) on top of Gold‑layer Lakehouse and Warehouse data products
Define DAX measures, hierarchies, relationships, and semantic model structures
Develop production Power BI dashboards for Trader P&L, Global Position, VaR, and Financial Consolidation
Work directly with trading, risk, and finance stakeholders to translate reporting requirements into semantic model design
Optimize DirectQuery and Import mode performance for complex financial and trading datasets
Define naming conventions, metric hierarchies, and semantic governance standards for the Fabric workspace
Collaborate with Data Engineers to ensure Gold‑layer outputs are analytics‑ready
Support self‑service analytics enablement for client analysts
Contribute to UAT, report validation, and business sign‑off on accuracy and performance


Requirements


7+ years in analytics engineering, BI development, or data analytics with production‑grade Power BI delivery
Deep hands‑on expertise with Power BI (semantic models, DAX, report design, workspace management, RLS)
Experience with Microsoft Fabric Power BI integration or SSAS/AAS as a semantic layer
Strong SQL for analytical queries and metric logic
Proven ability to engage finance, trading, or risk stakeholders and translate requirements into technical specifications
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative, technical, or analytical field (or equivalent experience)


Preferred Qualifications


Exposure to commodity trading, financial risk, or P&L reporting environments
Microsoft Power BI certification (PL‑300 or PL‑400)
Hands‑on experience with DAX optimization and performance tuning for large tabular models
Familiarity with Fabric Lakehouse/Warehouse as semantic model sources (DP‑600 or DP‑700 exposure a plus)


Salary: $ 5,500 - 7,200 [ USD ] monthly

Background checks may be required for certain positions/projects.

Blue Orange Digital is an equal-opportunity employer.

Title: Junior Associate OSINT (m/w/d)
Description:
Bernstein Group

Die Bernstein Group ist eine Unternehmensberatung für strategische Kommunikation mit einem Fokus auf kommunikative Sondersituationen, komplexe politische Entscheidungsprozesse und Regulierungsfragen. Seit unserer Gründung im Jahr 2001 haben wir uns stetig weiterentwickelt und sind heute mit einem 40-köpfigen Team an den Standorten Berlin, Brüssel, Frankfurt am Main und München vertreten. Wir arbeiten eng mit unseren Mandanten zusammen, entwickeln maßgeschneiderte Strategien und setzen diese erfolgreich um. Unsere Arbeit basiert auf fundierten Methoden, dem Einsatz von Technologie und datengetriebenen Analysen. Wir sind davon überzeugt, dass unsere Mitarbeiter:innen mit ihren vielfältigen Erfahrungen und Perspektiven ab dem ersten Tag einen wertvollen Beitrag leisten.

Wir suchen ab sofort am Standort Berlin, Frankfurt/Main, München oder Remote eine:n

Junior Associate OSINT (m/w/d)

Bei uns arbeitest du in einer etablierten Beratung an kommunikativen, politischen und regulatorischen Fragestellungen von hoher Relevanz. Du recherchierst, analysierst und verdichtest Informationen zu fundierten Entscheidungsgrundlagen und entwickelst gemeinsam mit dem Team kreative, umsetzbare Lösungsansätze. Neugier, analytisches Denken und Freude an vielfältigen Themen und Kontakten bringst du idealerweise mit.

Diese spannenden Aufgaben erwarten Dich:


Identifizierung, Sammlung und Analyse von Informationen aus öffentlich zugänglichen Quellen (OSINT)
Erstellung von Berichten und Analysen zur Unterstützung unserer Mandant:innen und deiner Kolleg:innen bei
der Einordnung von politischen, wirtschaftlichen und sozialen Entwicklungen
der Entwicklung und Umsetzung von Politik- und Kommunikationsstrategien
der Identifizierung von reputativen Risiken
im Litigation Support
Enge Zusammenarbeit mit dem Team, um datenbasierte Erkenntnisse in die Politik- und Kommunikationsstrategien unserer Mandant:innen zu integrieren
Weiterentwicklung unserer Daten-Infrastruktur, Unterstützung bei der (KI-basierten) Automatisierung unserer Arbeitsprozesse
Weiterentwicklung unseres Wissensmanagements


Was Du mitbringst:


Ein abgeschlossenes Hochschulstudium in einem relevanten Bereich (z.B. Data Science, Kommunikation, Politik, Journalismus oder Economics), eine vergleichbare Qualifikation oder relevante Arbeitserfahrung (z.B. im Journalismus oder in Sicherheitsbehörden)
Die Bereitschaft, dich in einem dynamischen Geschäftsumfeld immer wieder in neue Kontexte einzuarbeiten und das Wissen mit deinen Kolleg:innen zu teilen
Erste praktische Erfahrungen im Bereich von Open Source Intelligence oder anderen fortgeschrittenen Recherchetechniken
Erste praktische Erfahrungen im Einsatz von KI-Tools und gutes Verständnis der Herausforderungen durch KI-generierte Inhalte bei OSINT-Recherchen
Spaß daran, dich in neue Programme, Werkzeuge und Recherchetechniken einzuarbeiten
Interesse daran, Aufgaben und Prozesse zu automatisieren und skalierbar zu gestalten
Spaß an Komplexität: Du arbeitest gerne an Lösungen für vielschichtige Problemen in sensiblen Kontexten – auch mit engen Deadlines
Begeisterung für die Arbeit mit Sprache und Texten, den Mut zur prägnanten Sprache auf Deutsch und Englisch (C1-Niveau)
Kenntnisse über die Wechselwirkung von Politik, Medien, Wirtschaft, Wissenschaft und Zivilgesellschaft


Was wir bieten:


Ein wettbewerbsfähiges Gehalt mit einem attraktiven Bonussystem
Ein kluges Team mit anspruchsvollen und spannenden Mandaten
Die Möglichkeit, ab Tag 1 zum Erfolg beizutragen
Die Möglichkeit, Verantwortung für dich, dein Team und das Mandat zu übernehmen
Möglichkeit zu regelmäßigen Weiterbildungen


Neugier geweckt?

Dann freuen wir uns auf Deine Bewerbung (Motivationsschreiben, Lebenslauf, Zeugnis, ggf. Referenzen u.Ä.). Bei Fragen kannst du dich vorab gern per E-Mail unter recruiting@bernstein-group.com an uns wenden.

Title: Working Student Operations & Analytics (x/f/m)
Description:
Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Gesundheitsversorgung der Zukunft

Doctolib sucht Dich ab sofort als Werkstudent:in im Bereich Operations & Analytics für unseren Standort in Berlin.  

Das LOE-Team steht für operative Exzellenz quer durch unsere kundenseitigen Bereiche – von Deployment über Customer Onboarding und Customer Success bis hin zu Support Excellence. Wir stellen sicher, dass alle Teams mit sauberen Daten, verlässlichen KPI-Reportings und reibungslosen CRM-Prozessen arbeiten können. So tragen wir dazu bei, dass Tausende Gesundheitseinrichtungen in Deutschland täglich die bestmögliche Unterstützung von Doctolib erhalten – und leisten damit unseren direkten Beitrag zu unserer Mission: den Zugang zur Gesundheitsversorgung für alle zu verbessern.

Deine Aufgaben

Als Werkstudent:in unterstützt Du uns in den folgenden Aufgabenbereichen:

Wöchentliche Datenexporte aus Salesforce, Metabase & Google Sheets
Manuelle KPI-Updates in Qobra (unserem Tool zur Bonusberechnung)
Pflege von Abwesenheits- und Headcount-Tracking-Tabellen
Aktualisierung von KPI-Präsentationen für die Teams Customer Onboarding & Deployment
Erstellung wiederkehrender Salesforce-Cases (KYC, Preiserhöhung, Churn)
Unterstützung bei der Bonus-Validierung und der Vorbereitung von Performance Reviews
Formatierung & Re-Import von Daten zwischen verschiedenen Systemen (z. B. Typeform → Salesforce, Google Sheets → Salesforce)
Ad-hoc-Unterstützung bei Reporting-Anfragen und Analytics-Tickets
Beantwortung von Datenfragen quer durch alle Teams (KPI-Definitionen, Berechnungen)
Während Deiner Tätigkeit bei uns wirst Du lernen:
relevanten Business-Intelligence- und CRM-Tools (Salesforce, Metabase, Qobra) in einem schnell wachsenden HealthTech-Unternehmen
Ein tiefes Verständnis für datengetriebene Operations-Prozesse entwickeln und verstehen, wie KPI-Systeme in einer internationalen Organisation funktionieren
Cross-funktionale Zusammenarbeit in einem dynamischen Umfeld erleben und ein breites Netzwerk innerhalb von Doctolib aufbauen
Dein Profil

Du bist der festen Überzeugung, dass Du genau das mitbringst, was es für die Position braucht? Dann ermutigen wir Dich, Dich zu bewerben. Selbst wenn Du nicht jede Anforderung erfüllst, bist Du vielleicht genau die/der richtige zukünftige Doctoliber:in für diese oder andere Stellen!

Um Teil unseres Teams zu werden, bringst Du Folgendes mit:
Du bist in Vollzeit an einer Hochschule immatrikuliert und studierst im Bereich BWL, Wirtschaftsinformatik, Data Science, Statistik oder einem vergleichbaren Studiengang (ab dem 3. Semester)
Du hast sehr gute Kenntnisse in Google Sheets oder Excel (Formeln, strukturierte Daten) und bist sicher im Umgang mit strukturierten Datensätzen
Du arbeitest strukturiert, präzise und zuverlässig – auch bei wiederkehrenden Aufgaben
Du beherrschst die Datenformatierung, -bereinigung sowie den Import/Export zwischen verschiedenen Systemen sicher
Du kommunizierst klar auf Englisch (working level) und informierst Dein Team proaktiv bei Problemen oder Unklarheiten
Besonders interessant sind für uns außerdem:
Erste Erfahrungen mit Salesforce (Exporte, Case-Erstellung, grundlegende Navigation)
Kenntnisse in Metabase oder einem ähnlichen BI-Tool
Deutschkenntnisse von Vorteil
Was wir Dir bieten
Ein strukturiertes und individuelles Onboarding im Rahmen unserer Doctolib Academy sowie ein breites Angebot interner Trainingsprogramme
Ein von Doctolib gesponsertes Deutschlandticket oder die Übernahme Deines Semestertickets
28 Urlaubstage im Jahr (anteilig auf Deine Arbeitszeiten berechnet)
Ein flexibles Arbeitsmodell, das hybrides und Office-basiertes Arbeiten ermöglicht
Regelmäßige Frühstücksangebote im Büro sowie Bezuschussungen zu Deinem Lebensmitteleinkauf
Ein gefördertes Sportangebot mit unserem Partner Urban Sports Club
Der Interviewprozess
Interview mit einer Deiner Managerin
Case Study mit Deinen Teammitgliedern
Offer
Job Details
Tätigkeit als Werkstudent:in (20 Std./Woche)
Tätigkeitsdauer: 3 Monate
Arbeitsort: Berlin
Gehalt: 15 €/Std.
Startdatum: zum nächstmöglichen Zeitpunkt
Arbeitsmodus: Hybrid (2-3 Tage Office/Woche)



Wir verfolgen die Vision, die Gesundheit aller zu verbessern. Dies spiegelt sich auch im Recruiting wider. Wir evaluieren Bewerbungen ausschließlich auf der Grundlage von Qualifikation und Motivation. Diskriminierung wird bei uns nicht toleriert.

Je diverser unser Team und die Ideen sind, desto mehr wird unser Produkt die Gesundheit unserer Nutzer:innen verbessern können. Deshalb laden wir dich ein, dich bei uns zu bewerben, unabhängig von Geschlecht, Religion, Alter, sexueller Orientierung, ethnischer Herkunft oder Behinderung. 

Um Chancengleichheit zu gewährleisten, kannst Du in deiner Bewerbung auf Angaben wie Fotos, Alter, etc. verzichten. Lass uns bitte wissen, welche Vorkehrungen wir treffen können, um Dich bestmöglich zu unterstützen. Wir freuen uns, mit Dir das Gesundheitswesen zu digitalisieren!

Alle bereitgestellten Informationen werden von Doctolib für das Bewerbungsmanagement verarbeitet. Für Details zur Datenverarbeitung klicke bitte hier. Bei Fragen oder wenn Du Deine Rechte wahrnehmen möchtest, wende Dich bitte an hr.dataprivacy(at)doctolib.com.

Title: Robotics Data Collector (Germany)
Description:
CNTXT AI is a leading artificial intelligence company focused on building next-generation AI systems, products, and infrastructure. We specialize in developing multimodal AI, robotics, and intelligent agents, powered by high-quality real-world data. Data is at the core of our innovation. We invest heavily in data collection, annotation, and AI training pipelines to enable advanced systems such as embodied AI and Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models.

Role Summary

CNTXT AI is looking for a Robotics Data Collector to support the collection of high-quality robotics and egocentric data used to train AI models.
This role involves working hands-on with robotic systems, sensors, and real-world environments to capture multimodal datasets, including video, motion, and interaction data. You will play a key role in enabling the development of intelligent robotic systems and AI agents.
The ideal candidate is detail-oriented, technically curious, and comfortable working in lab and controlled real-world environments.

Key Responsibilities

1. Data Collection Execution

Collect robotics and egocentric (first-person) data using:

Cameras (handheld, head-mounted, or robot-mounted)

Sensors (IMU, depth cameras, etc.)

Execute data collection tasks based on predefined scenarios and protocols.

Ensure consistent and accurate recording of actions, interactions, and environments.





2. Robotics Operation Support

Assist in operating robotic systems (humanoid or mobile robots) during data collection.

Support setup and calibration of:

Cameras and sensors

Recording systems

Ensure proper functioning of hardware during experiments.





3. Data Quality & Validation

Review collected data for:

Completeness

Clarity

Accuracy

Identify and flag issues such as:

Missing data

Recording errors

Sensor misalignment

Follow quality guidelines to ensure high usability for AI training.





4. Experiment Setup & Execution

Prepare environments for data collection sessions.

Execute tasks such as:

Object manipulation

Navigation scenarios

Human-robot interaction recordings

Follow detailed instructions to simulate real-world use cases.





5. Documentation & Reporting

Maintain records of:

Data collection sessions

Equipment usage

Issues encountered

Provide feedback to improve data collection processes and protocols.





6. Safety & Compliance

Follow all lab safety protocols and operational guidelines.

Handle robotics equipment and sensors with care.

Ensure compliance with data privacy and security requirements.





Required Qualifications


0–3+ years of experience

Data collection, field operations, or lab environments

Technical or hardware-related roles

Comfortable working with:

Cameras, sensors, and recording devices

Basic computer systems and tools

Strong attention to detail and ability to follow instructions precisely.





Preferred Skills

Experience with robotics systems or hardware environments.

Familiarity with:

Egocentric or first-person data collection

Computer vision or AI-related datasets

Basic understanding of:

Robotics concepts

Sensors and data recording tools

Experience working in technical labs or research environments.





Key Traits

Detail-oriented and quality-focused

Hands-on and execution-driven

Reliable and consistent in following protocols

Comfortable working in dynamic environments

Curious about robotics and AI





Success Metrics

Volume and quality of data collected

Accuracy and completeness of datasets

Adherence to data collection protocols

Reduction in data errors and rework

Efficiency in executing data collection tasks





Why Join CNTXT AI

Work on cutting-edge robotics and AI systems

Contribute to building next-generation intelligent agents

Gain hands-on experience with robotics labs and real-world data

Be part of a fast-growing, innovation-driven company

Title: Werkstudent:in Data Science
Description:
Über nerou




Die Datenanalyse-Software von nerou unterstützt Betreiber von Kläranlagen bei täglichen Entscheidungen in der Anlagensteuerung. In Kläranlagen müssen komplexe Entscheidungen getroffen werden, um die Anlage auf sich täglich ändernde Einflüsse einzustellen. Alle relevanten Daten werden von Machine Learning Algorithmen analysiert und ausgewertet. Die gewonnen Erkenntnisse werden in Form von Entscheidungshilfen an den Kunden weitergeleitet. Dies reduziert nicht nur Kosten für die Betreiber, sondern schont so auch Ressourcen und Umwelt. Zur Verstärkung unseres Data Science-Bereichs suchen wir nun Dich!







Deine Tätigkeiten



Du extrahierst und validierst große Datenbestände auf Grundlage verschiedener Fragestellungen
Du analysierst Datensätze mit geeigneten statistischen Methoden
Du ergänzt unser motiviertes Team mit Machine Learning- und Data Science-Expertise






Deine Erfahrung

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About Huzzle

At Huzzle, we connect exceptional talents with top opportunities at leading companies across the UK, US, Canada, Europe, and Australia. Our clients include startups, digital agencies, and tech platforms in industries such as SaaS, MarTech, FinTech, and AI.

Unlike an outsourcing agency, we place you directly with a client where you're hired in-house as a valued member of their team.

Job Type: Full-time

Location: On-site in Berlin, Germany

About The Company

Our client is an ambitious, venture-backed startup building an AI-powered SAP consultant designed to transform how enterprise systems are implemented and optimized. Already post-revenue and working with DAX 40 and Fortune 500 companies. Their mission is to encode and compound consulting knowledge across deployments, creating a system that becomes smarter with every customer engagement.

Job Summary

We are hiring an AI Data Engineer to design and build the foundational data layer that powers a next-generation AI system for SAP consulting. This role is critical in structuring complex enterprise data—capturing configurations, decisions, and relationships across deployments—and transforming it into scalable, queryable knowledge that improves model accuracy over time.

You will work at the intersection of data engineering and AI, ensuring that structured insights flow seamlessly into machine learning systems and continuously evolve with each customer interaction.

Key Responsibilities


Design and build a scalable data layer that maps relationships across SAP projects, including configurations, outcomes, and domain patterns
Own the metadata architecture, including data labeling, linking, versioning, and queryability
Develop robust data pipelines to ingest SAP telemetry and consulting insights into structured datasets
Collaborate closely with AI/ML teams to provide high-quality signals for model training and inference
Continuously refine and improve data systems with each customer deployment
Ensure high standards of data integrity, consistency, and usability across systems
Architect systems with a long-term, scalable mindset rather than short-term fixes


Requirements


Strong background in data engineering, including pipeline development, schema design, and data modeling
Experience working with complex, semi-structured enterprise data
Knowledge of graph-based systems (knowledge graphs, graph databases) or relational modeling for complex domains
Systems-level thinking with a focus on architecture and scalability
Proven ability to maintain high standards for data quality and reliability
Willingness to work on-site in Berlin (mandatory requirement)


Benefits

💰 Competitive salary aligned with top-tier European startup benchmarks

🌍 Work on-site in Berlin at a fast-growing, venture-backed AI company

📈 High-growth environment with strong funding and enterprise traction

🤝 Collaborate with elite engineers, AI researchers, and enterprise clients

🧠 Exposure to cutting-edge AI, data systems, and enterprise-scale challenges
Show more

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Customer Care Analytics
Description:
Change the way the world travels

Join the GetYourGuide journey to connect people with unforgettable travel experiences around the world. Millions look to us for unique activities they can trust, and it’s all powered by our commitment to make every single journey extraordinary - including yours.

Ready to unlock your potential with a community of fellow explorers? Find your next role at our Berlin HQ or one of our local offices around the globe, from New York to Bangkok. Head to getyourguide.careers to take the first step.

Team mission

In this analytics role, you will be the key analytical partner to Customer Care — the team that supports travelers when things don’t go as planned and protects trust in the GetYourGuide experience. You will work closely with Care leadership, Product, and the broader Analytics organization to ensure Care’s biggest decisions are grounded in rigorous measurement and clear trade-offs.

Care is inherently cross-domain: it spans Traveler (On-Trip), Supplier, and Partner Operations. You’ll be embedded enough to build context and influence, while staying anchored in Analytics to uphold standards, peer review, and alignment with company-wide metrics.

Your mission


Own the analytical problems that matter most for Care — define and drive the strategic Care analytics backlog and turn it into recommendations that shape priorities, policies, and investments.
Build the measurement frameworks Care relies on — create and evolve metrics, models, and dashboards that make efficiency, friction, and outcomes measurable and self-serve.
Deliver deep-dive modeling that unlocks step-change decisions — quantify drivers and trade-offs behind contacts, refunds, CSAT, and channel performance, including LTV-aware policy logic.
Be Care's analytical voice across the company for Travelers, Suppliers and Partners — turn what Care learns from travelers into insights Product, Marketing, and Operations can act on, aligned with On-Trip and Destination growth strategy.
Champion hypothesis-driven decision-making — raise the bar on rigor, evaluation, and impact measurement so Care decisions are consistently defensible and outcome-driven.


Your toolkit


5+ years of relevant experience interpreting data in an analytics or a data science role
Production-grade SQL and strong working knowledge of Python (e.g., notebooks, modeling, analysis workflows).
Experience with experimentation (statistics, assumptions, and evaluation).
Strong statistical background and proficiency with advanced statistical methods (e.g. causal inference methods, difference-in-differences, regression analysis, propensity score matching)
Strong stakeholder management and the ability to operate with senior leaders in a high-context, ambiguous environment.
Proven ability to turn messy questions into structured analysis, crisp narratives, and decisions with measurable business impact.
Strong customer mindset: you understand user behavior and the “why” behind the numbers.
Excellent communication skills — you can influence without authority and align teams around shared metrics and priorities.
Experience with data visualization and reporting tools (e.g., Looker, Jupyter Notebooks).


Extras that give you an edge


Experience working in Customer Care within a marketplace.
Familiarity with ETL/orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow) and/or big data tooling (e.g., Spark, Hive).
Experience working in agile product teams and cross-functional mission setups.
Exposure to LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic), especially for operational tooling or workflow augmentation.
Experience mentoring or managing analysts.


How We’ll Make Your Career Journey Extraordinary


Annual personal growth budget and mentorship programs for continuous learning and development
Work from anywhere in the world for 30 days per year
A hybrid working approach with three days of in office collaboration (Mon, Tue, Thur) and two days of optional at home focus time.
Opportunities to collaborate and socialize with team members through quarterly team events and yearly company-wide events
Monthly transportation and fitness budget
Discounts for you, your friends, and family on GetYourGuide activities
Language reimbursement program
Health and wellness benefits


And more…

How To Apply

Submit your CV/resume in English using the form below. For tips and insights into our hiring process and culture, check out ‘how we hire’ and ‘life at GetYourGuide’. If you have any further questions, please don’t hesitate to get in touch at jobs@getyourguide.com.

We’re an equal opportunities employer

Our commitment is that every qualified person will be evaluated according to their skills regardless of age, gender identity, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability status, or religion. Please refrain from including your picture and age with your application.

Title: Werkstudent Business Intelligence (m/w/d)
Description:
About MYNE

Wir sind MYNE Homes - der cleverste Weg in die eigenen Traum-Ferienimmobilie. Mit unserem innovativen Co-Ownership Angebot geben wir mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit, sich ihren Traum vom eigenen Ferienhaus zu erfüllen. Gegründet von Serial Entrepreneurs und unterstützt von einigen der renommiertesten Business Angels in Europa (Gründer von Auto1, Smava und Numa) wachsen wir schnell und suchen dich um uns zu unterstützen das nächste Proptech Unicorn aufzubauen!

Als Werkstudent Business Intelligence (m/w/d) unterstützt Du die Super-User unseres Marketingteams und sammelst dabei wertvolle Erfahrungen in einem dynamischen Unternehmensumfeld. Diese Position bietet Dir eine hervorragende Gelegenheit, praxisnahe Fähigkeiten in der Datenanalyse, Business Intelligence und im Bereich Marketing Analytics zu entwickeln.

Deine Aufgaben


Du wirst ab Tag eins vollwertiges Mitglied des Business Intelligence-Teams mit Zugang zum Data Warehouse und allen relevanten Tools.
Du unterstützt die Super-User im Marketingteam bei Datenanalysen, Reportings und wöchentlichen Insights.
Du lernst neue Tools und Technologien kennen und bleibst nah an aktuellen Business-Entwicklungen.
Du übernimmst mit wachsender Erfahrung mehr Verantwortung, z. B. bei Code Reviews.


Dein Profil


Du studierst Wirtschaftswissenschaften, (Wirtschafts-)Informatik oder einen vergleichbaren Studiengang.
Du bist an einer Berliner Hochschule eingeschrieben und hast noch mindestens 12 Monate bis zur Abschlussarbeit.
Du bist 16–20 Stunden pro Woche verfügbar, idealerweise an 2–3 festen Wochentagen.
Du lernst schnell und passt Dich gut an ein dynamisches Umfeld an.
idealerweise beherrschst du G-Sheets und Excel, inklusive Funktionen wie VLOOKUP und Pivot-Tabellen.
Du sprichst fließend Englisch und sehr gut Deutsch.


Kein Muss Aber Von Vorteil


Du hast erste Erfahrungen mit SQL, dbt und/oder version control tools wie Git.
Du kennst Dich mit Performance-Marketing-Kanälen wie Google Ads, Meta o. ä. aus.
Du hast bereits in schnelllebigen Umfeldern wie Startups, Beratung, Investment Banking oder Private Equity Praktika gearbeitet.


Warum wir?


Die Chance eines der führenden Proptech-Startups aktiv mit aufzubauen und zu gestalten.
Du arbeitest direkt mit erfahrenen Gründern und hochkarätigen Investoren aus der Technologie- und Immobilienwelt zusammen.
Verantwortungsvolle und abwechslungsreiche Aufgaben mit Freiraum für eigene Ideen.
Motiviertes und ergebnisorientiertes Team, flache Hierarchien und kurze Entscheidungswege.
Kompetitives Gehalt und die üblichen Startup Benefits.
Und natürlich: Aufenthalte in unseren Traum-Ferienimmobilien!


Über uns

Unsere Mission

Wir sind MYNE Homes - der cleverste Weg in die eigene Traum-Ferienimmobilie. Mit unserem innovativen Co-Ownership Angebot geben wir mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit, sich ihren Traum vom eigenen Ferienhaus zu erfüllen. Gegründet von Serial Entrepreneurs und unterstützt von einigen der renommiertesten Business Angels in Europa (Gründer von Auto1, Smava und Numa), wachsen wir schnell und suchen dich, um gemeinsam das nächste Proptech Unicorn aufzubauen!

Was treibt uns an

Wir wollen mehr Menschen die Möglichkeit geben, sich ihren Traum von der eigenen Ferienimmobilie zu erfüllen.

Title: Sr. Digital Analyst – Tesla Energy EMEA (m/w/d)
Description:
What To Expect
Please submit your CV in English in order to be considered for the role.

Data is deeply embedded in the product, engineering, quality, and customer experience culture at Tesla. This role will collaborate across Demand Generation, Marketing, Web Development, Software, and executive teams to optimize the Web and Digital Customer Experience for Tesla Energy for all of Europe.

This position requires an in-depth understanding of digital marketing best practices, web UI/UX, web traffic data, web marketing channels, first through third party data, and data analysis.

Web Digital Platforms Analyst at Tesla supports campaign planning, site optimization, campaign performance, and data activation.

What You'll Do


Lead web analytics for Tesla Energy in Europe, optimizing customer conversion and interaction
Design Tests and Audit performance in paid media campaigns (Google, Meta, X, and others)
Design and conduct A/B tests on web pages to evaluate performance and drive improvements
Analyze web traffic data to recommend UX enhancements and ensure tagging/tracking compliance
Combine marketing channel data with CRM insights for audience sizing and campaign optimization
Deploy dashboards and conduct impact analysis of business process changes on customer experience
Understand multi-channel marketing campaigns and develop explanatory performance models



What You'll Bring


Relevant enterprise-level experience in web analytics, digital marketing, or data science
Business-level communication skills in German and English (Italian a plus)
Expertise in BigQuery, GA4 funnels, A/B test configuration, and Google Tag Manager
Proficiency in statistical tools (significance tests, regression, time series analysis)
Strong ability to use BI tools (Looker Studio, Tableau, PowerBI) and write SQL
Experience blending web data with CRM/databases across multiple systems
Bonus: Familiarity with UX design, Design Thinking, and data taxonomy (DMP/CDP), Optimizely

Title: AI Data Engineer
Description:
About Qorelo

Qorelo uses AI to automate some of the most mission critical work in the enterprise: the management of SAP systems. SAP runs the core of the world's largest companies, including finance, supply chain, and operations, yet keeping it modern is slow, manual, and consultant heavy. We're changing that.

We're moving at lightning speed to revolutionize how enterprises run their IT stack, starting with SAP, the heart of many enterprises. Without a strong base system, innovation can never truly succeed. Our




AI agents help teams integrate, migrate, and modernize SAP faster and with fewer errors, compressing work that used to take months. We've raised $3.5M from HPI Ventures, the fund tied to the institute of SAP co-founder Hasso Plattner, along with many other great backers. We already work with Fortune 500 and DAX 40 companies, including Mercedes-Benz.




Our promise

Our team is made up of true innovators, builders, and entrepreneurs. What we offer is an environment to grow, with real ownership of not just your day to day work but the direction of the company itself. We're looking for people who want to shape a company, not just join one.




What You'll Own

Design and build the data layer that maps relationships across SAP projects: configurations, outcomes, domain patterns
Own our metadata architecture, including how data is labelled, linked, versioned, and made queryable
Build pipelines that ingest SAP telemetry and consulting learnings and output clean, structured knowledge
Work with the AI team to feed the right signals into the models
Improve the system with every customer and every deployment




What You Need

Strong data engineering background across pipelines, schema design, and data modelling
Experience with knowledge graphs, graph databases, or relational modelling of complex domain data
Comfortable with large, messy, semi structured data from enterprise systems
Systems level thinker who designs architecture first, not just query by query
High standards for data quality




Nice to Have

Familiarity with SAP data models or ERP data structures
Experience with embedding pipelines, vector stores, or LLM data infrastructure

HTML:
About Qorelo

Qorelo uses AI to automate some of the most mission critical work in the enterprise: the management of SAP systems. SAP runs the core of the world's largest companies, including finance, supply chain, and operations, yet keeping it modern is slow, manual, and consultant heavy. We're changing that.

We're moving at lightning speed to revolutionize how enterprises run their IT stack, starting with SAP, the heart of many enterprises. Without a strong base system, innovation can never truly succeed. Our


AI agents help teams integrate, migrate, and modernize SAP faster and with fewer errors, compressing work that used to take months. We've raised $3.5M from HPI Ventures, the fund tied to the institute of SAP co-founder Hasso Plattner, along with many other great backers. We already work with Fortune 500 and DAX 40 companies, including Mercedes-Benz.


Our promise

Our team is made up of true innovators, builders, and entrepreneurs. What we offer is an environment to grow, with real ownership of not just your day to day work but the direction of the company itself. We're looking for people who want to shape a company, not just join one.


What You'll Own

  • Design and build the data layer that maps relationships across SAP projects: configurations, outcomes, domain patterns
  • Own our metadata architecture, including how data is labelled, linked, versioned, and made queryable
  • Build pipelines that ingest SAP telemetry and consulting learnings and output clean, structured knowledge
  • Work with the AI team to feed the right signals into the models
  • Improve the system with every customer and every deployment


What You Need

  • Strong data engineering background across pipelines, schema design, and data modelling
  • Experience with knowledge graphs, graph databases, or relational modelling of complex domain data
  • Comfortable with large, messy, semi structured data from enterprise systems
  • Systems level thinker who designs architecture first, not just query by query
  • High standards for data quality


Nice to Have

  • Familiarity with SAP data models or ERP data structures
  • Experience with embedding pipelines, vector stores, or LLM data infrastructure

Title: Web Analyst (m/f/d) - Marketing & Data
Description:
We are home24, one of the leading home & living e-commerce platforms in continental Europe. We utilize data to provide personalized recommendations, tips, and inspiration for every home. We are looking for a Web Analyst (m/f/d) to join our team. In this role, you will support our marketing teams by ensuring data quality, maintaining our tracking setup, and delivering actionable insights through data visualization.

Your Responsibilities


You drive the implementation and maintenance of tracking concepts in GA4 and sGTM, refining event structures and debugging discrepancies to ensure delivery of high-quality data e.g. for attribution modeling and the design and evaluation of A/B tests
You evaluate the performance of marketing campaigns across various channels
You don't just deliver numbers, but interpret data to help the team understand trends and optimize ROI
You build and maintain interactive dashboards (e.g., in Tableau or Looker) to monitor key marketing metrics, translating business requirements into clear visualizations
You look beyond standard KPIs to investigate the "why" behind the data, conducting ad-hoc deep dives to uncover the root causes of performance shifts and user behavior
You act as a contact person for analytical questions regarding GA4, helping stakeholders understand their data usage needs
You monitor the data flow to ensure accuracy and support the team in identifying and fixing bugs in the tracking landscape


Your Profile


You have professional experience in web analytics, data analysis, or a related field, ideally within an e-commerce environment
You have hands-on experience with Google Analytics 4 (GA4) and (server-side) Google Tag Manager (sGTM)
You are proficient in SQL, capable of merging and transforming large-scale datasets from diverse sources. Proficiency in Python is a strong plus for building scalable data solutions
You have a basic understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to comprehend how tracking is implemented
Your mindset combines natural curiosity for new trends with a sharp eye for detail, ensuring your end-to-end thinking always aligns with the bigger picture
You have excellent English skills and can communicate your findings in a clear, structured way
You thrive in cross-functional collaboration, acting as a bridge between marketing, data, and tech teams


What We Offer You

Your impact


The chance to make an impact in a highly motivated team of professionals
An open-minded culture ready to incorporate new and creative ideas


Your growth


Extensive learning opportunities from our home24 academy and external partners
Room for growth and development within your role through regular feedback rounds


Your path


Hybrid working model: you have the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays in our standard working mode. We are in the office together Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays
The possibility to work up to two months per year within Germany or from an EU country
Up to 30 days paid vacation each year to unwind and recharge
City mobility with a subsidized public transportation ticket and our eco-friendly bike leasing program #ridinghome


Your second home


International campus in the heart of Berlin featuring drive-in bicycle garage, outdoor space and lounge areas, fitness facilities, parent and multi-faith rooms close to the Berlin S-Bahn train station Landsberger Allee
Company benefits including a pension scheme, 30 % off the home24 assortment, and employee discounts at partner organizations
You want to learn more about our home24 company culture and what it is like to be a homie? Follow @teamhome24 on Instagram


If you want to become part of a thrilling success story – we are looking forward to your application. Please send us your application together with your salary expectations and the earliest possible starting date via our career site.

Your home is surely filled with furniture of varying materials and styles. The same is true for home24 as an employer. We celebrate the diversity of the people who work with us. Be who you are and feel welcome to #makeyourselfathome24

You can find all job offers on our career site !

home24 SE | Otto-Ostrowski-Straße 3 | 10249 Berlin | Contact: Nadine Scharf

We are home24, the leading home & living e-commerce platform in continental Europe. We are currently active in seven countries. Over 1,000 Homies work together towards a common goal: to create happy homes for everyone and every budget.

How can we reach this goal? With an assortment of over 250,000 pieces of furniture and accessories from more than 1,000 suppliers, we offer the perfect addition to any living space combined with the comfort of being able to shop from anywhere.

What makes us proud to be Homies? We are pioneers in our market who seamlessly combine technology and creativity. We utilize data to provide personalized recommendations, tips and inspiration for every home.

At home24, we strive to deliver a superior assortment of products and the best service. To do this, we need fresh ideas, 100% motivation, and dedication to our company mission. We always have a space at our table for service-minded individuals who are passionate about digitization and modern living.

Title: Junior Tracking Specialist (all genders)
Description:
Your role

As our (Junior) Tracking Specialist you will support the measurement of user behavior and E-Commerce data on our websites and possible future app(s). For this, you will help continuously evolve the client- and server-side Google Tag Manager setup, including the usage of accompanying features in Google Cloud, as well as improving the GA4 setup.

The position centers around supporting to set up the best possible tracking to provide high-quality data to be used in our marketing channels, for website optimization, and in BI reporting to facilitate further business growth.

You will be an important contributor to the company's website data setup for our 10+ country domains together with our Senior Tracking Specialist.


Continuously improve the quality and quantity of measured data in GTM and GA 4
Support implementing data quality monitoring measures and creating suitable reports with Looker Studio
Participate in shaping our measurement strategy to meet marketing and business needs
Take over parts of the maintenance of Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics
Educate stakeholders about the features and limitations of our tracking setup
Continuously monitor new developments in the digital analytics ecosphere
Collaborate closely with our marketing and data teams to solve business questions, create synergies, and drive business growth through data
Support marketing teams with requests


Your profile


At least 1 year of in-depth experience with Google Tag Manager and Google Analytics 4; App tracking is a bonus
Demonstrated success in the development of tracking setups
Experience with setting up Server-Side GTM in a multi-domain setup
You are accustomed to the tracking needs of paid channels such as Google Ads, Meta Ads, and others
Basic knowledge in SQL, CSS, and JS is a plus
Strong focus on structured approaches, documentation, and organizing maintenance and monitoring of data quality
High interest in closely collaborating with Data and Marketing Teams
Talent to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
An interest in how AI will augment work in data measurement and digital analytics is a plus


What We Offer


An appealing discount on all of our products – from essential oils to vitamins & nutrients
We offer 28 days of paid time off per year plus up to 5 days off between Christmas and New Year
The Urban Sports Club membership and either Swapfiets bike rental or a subsidy for the Deutschlandticket
We believe great things happen when we meet in person, which is why we embrace a hybrid model with one home office day per week – up to two depending on the area you are working in – and up to two weeks per year working from abroad
Receive various discounts of up to more than 50 percent on attractive brands such as Flaconi, Bosch, Apple or Expedia
You will be given real ownership and plenty of room for new initiatives, as well as the opportunity to contribute your expertise and implement your great ideas
Inviting office spaces with access to plenty of organic teas and superfoods from around the world
We like to celebrate each other! Regular tea & office parties, Christmas and summer feasts and various influencer events such as Women's Day


Apply with your CV and cover letter.

Sunday Natural emerged from a long-standing passion and research in the fields of health, healing, and personal growth. The lack of natural, high-quality products on the market was the original motivation for founding Sunday Natural in 2013. We aim to produce the products we ourselves would love to buy – as natural as possible, absolutely pure, free from any additives, and of the highest quality. In a strategic partnership with CVC, Sunday Natural is now one of Germany's most renowned quality manufacturers, with its own research and development department in Berlin. For the continuous improvement of our products, we collaborate with selected research institutes, scientists, therapists, farmers, and professional associations.

We take our ecological responsibility very seriously: that’s why the majority of our product range is vegan. We also take an innovative approach to packaging: most of our products are packaged in glass, making them almost entirely recyclable.

Our diverse team in Berlin consists of over 350 employees from more than 45 nationalities and continues to grow – but at a sustainable pace. Our goal is to foster a collaborative, supportive, and inclusive work environment with a diverse group of motivated, talented, and visionary team members.

We highly value equality of opportunity and inclusivity, and we would like to particularly encourage women and candidates from under-represented backgrounds to apply, even if you don’t match with 100% of the requirements.

Title: Reporting Process & Analytics Expert EQS (all genders), befristet bis 31.03.2027
Description:
CDO Expert Analysis & Reporting (all genders), befristet bis zum 31.03.2027, HQ-Berlin

Ohne uns gibt’s weder Coke, Fanta noch Sprite im Supermarktregal. Klingt verrückt, ist aber so.

Denn wir sind Coca‑Cola Europacific Partners Deutschland (CCEP) und verantworten bundesweit die Abfüllung und den Vertrieb von Coca‑Cola Markenprodukten.

Und damit das auch weiterhin so reibungslos funktioniert, brauchen wir Menschen wie dich im Bereich Cold Drink Equipment! Unser Team soll nicht nur größer, sondern auch bunter werden. Es ist also egal, woher du kommst, woran du glaubst oder welches Geschlecht du hast. Bei uns zählt einzig und allein deine Persönlichkeit und deine Motivation.

Davon hast du mehr als genug? Dann werde Teil von Deutschlands größtem Getränkehersteller und gestalte mit uns datenbasierte Entscheidungen für die Zukunft.

Was wir dir bieten

Planungssicherheit:

Mit flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und verlässlicher Planung kannst du Beruf und Familie problemlos miteinander vereinbaren. So behältst du nicht nur deinen Job im Blick, sondern auch dein Privatleben.

Persönliche Weiterentwicklung:

Wir fordern dich nicht nur, sondern fördern dich auch. Mit einer Vielzahl an Netzwerkevents und Förderprogrammen, wie z.B. „Cross-Mentoring“ unterstützen wir deine individuelle Entwicklung. Bei uns steht deine Individualität im Mittelpunkt.      

Aktionär werden:

Wir belohnen deine Investition in unser Unternehmen, indem du für jede Aktie, die du kaufst und 12 Monate lang behältst, kostenlos eine Zusatzaktie von uns bekommst.

Dein Profil

Wir suchen Menschen, keine Nummern. Es ist also egal, ob dein Lebenslauf lückenlos ist oder nicht. Bei uns zählen Persönlichkeit und Motivation. Also keine Sorge, wenn du nicht alle Anforderungen mitbringst.


Mehrjährige Erfahrung im Bereich Analyse, Reporting oder Controlling
Abgeschlossenes Studium oder vergleichbare Qualifikation mit starkem Zahlenverständnis
Sehr gute Kenntnisse in Excel sowie idealerweise Power BI, SAP oder ähnlichen Tools
Erfahrung in Projektmanagement, Prozessoptimierung und datenbasierter Steuerung


Deine Aufgaben


Erstellung und Analyse von CDO‑Reports sowie Ableitung von Handlungsempfehlungen für das Business
Durchführung von Soll‑Ist‑Vergleichen und KPI‑Tracking zur Steuerung der Performance
Identifikation und Umsetzung digitaler Lösungen zur Effizienzsteigerung im Equipment‑Umfeld
Steuerung und Umsetzung nationaler und internationaler Projekte mit Fokus auf Kostenoptimierung und Prozessverbesserung


Was du sonst noch wissen solltest:

Wir möchten die Stelle gerne schnellstmöglich besetzten. Sie erfordert eine Büro-Präsenz von drei Tagen pro Woche in Berlin und zudem eine Reisebereitschaft von bis zu 50 %

Title: Junior Researcher (f/m/d)
Company: ISDC - International Security and Development Center
HTML:
We are looking for

Junior Researcher (f/m/d)

(full-time - 40 hours per week)

for our Welfare Research Program, starting 1 August 2026. The Welfare Research Program at ISDC focuses on the intersection between humanitarian emergencies, fragility, and conflict on one hand and economic and social well-being on the other. We expand the state of the art by designing and conducting experimental and quasi-experimental studies to understand how households live and cope with shocks. This informs our understanding of how humanitarian and development programs can help alleviate vulnerable households from poverty and strengthen their resilience and food and nutritional security. The Junior Researcher will primarily contribute to a project measuring resilience impacts of a multi-sectoral humanitarian program in Syria.

Your Responsibilities

  • Supporting the coordination of quantitative micro-level survey data collection with several implementing partners;
  • Cleaning, matching, and analyzing quantitative micro-level data;
  • Conducting literature review; and
  • Contributing to the writing of reports and papers.

Qualificatons and key competencies

  • An excellent Master’s degree in economics or social sciences;
  • Very strong skills in statistical analysis and data management in R;
  • Experience in developing and managing surveys using ODK;
  • Experience in working with large quantitative micro-level data;
  • Experience working in and on fragile and conflict-affected settings (desirable, but not necessary); and
  • Excellent written and oral English-language skills (additional knowledge of Arabic and German is desirable, but not necessary).

What You’ll Gain By Working With Us

  • Work on pressing global challenges with interesting partners, donors, and networks;
  • A diverse, creative, and collaborative working environment in the center of Berlin;
  • A one-year contract with remuneration at a monthly gross salary of 3,341 EUR;
  • A flexible work model, incl. the opportunity to work from abroad for 3 weeks per year; and
  • 30 days of annual leave and additional days off on 24 and 31 of December.

Interested?

Then please apply! Via the respective fields on our website you can upload your:

  • CV;
  • A cover letter (maximum one page) explaining why you would be a great fit for this position;
  • Your certificates and transcripts of records from the Bachelors and Masters degrees; and
  • Two academic or professional recommendation letters or, if not available, the contact details for two references.

Incomplete applications will not be considered.

The final deadline for applications is 5 July 2026, though interviews may be held as applications are received. We kindly ask candidates to refrain from proactively asking for updates on their application.

We are looking forward to hearing from you soon!

About Us

ISDC – International Security and Development Center is a non-profit academic institute based in Berlin, Germany. We believe in the power of data to study the impact of violent conflict, humanitarian emergencies, and fragility on lives and livelihoods. You can find out more about us and our work by visiting www.isdc.org.

Title: NGS Data Analyst for Molecular Oncology
Description:
Alacris Theranostics GmbH is a leader in innovative molecular diagnostics, specialising in personalised oncology. Our cutting-edge solutions combine next-generation sequencing (NGS), advanced bioinformatics, and systems biology to transform scientific insights into enhanced cancer diagnostics and more precise treatment decisions. We collaborate closely with leading research institutions, hospitals, and pharmaceutical partners to drive meaningful and lasting improvements in patient outcomes.


Tasks


We are seeking an experienced Bioinformatician / Computational Biologist with a strong track record in next-generation sequencing (NGS) data analysis. The successful candidate will be responsible for processing and analysing NGS datasets - including RNA-Seq, whole-exome sequencing (WES), whole-genome sequencing (WGS), and single-cell sequencing - derived from tumour samples.


Requirements


The ideal candidate will demonstrate excellent programming skills in R and Python, along with proven expertise in NGS data analysis and a solid foundation in human molecular genetics. Knowledge of statistics, high-dimensional data analysis, and cancer genomics is highly desirable.




We are looking for a candidate with a genuine interest in precision oncology, a proactive approach to learning, and a commitment to developing new skills. Strong motivation, a collaborative mindset, and excellent communication skills are essential


Benefits


You will join a multidisciplinary team of bioinformaticians, molecular biologists, and software engineers dedicated to developing innovative analysis pipelines and diagnostic solutions in oncology.




We offer a rewarding and challenging role within a dynamic biotech company based in the vibrant city of Berlin. The position comes with a competitive salary and a stimulating working environment. The appointment is available immediately and will initially be for a two-year term, with the possibility of extension.




How to Apply? Interested candidates should provide a CV, publication list, details of previous research and work experience, and contact details of three referees as a single PDF file, citing reference ALA-NGSA-0426.




The closing date for applications is April 15, 2026.
