Title: Process Analyst, EU Central Operations Analytics
Description:
Description

Amazon Logistics (AMZL) is transforming the future of last-mile delivery through Central Operations, a strategic initiative that's revolutionizing how we approach route planning, scheduling, and delivery execution. We are seeking a Process Analyst to join our EU Central Operations team, where you'll play a pivotal role in driving AI-powered automation and enhancing our delivery network operations through advanced analytics and intelligent process improvements.

This role uniquely combines deep business operations knowledge with hands-on technical implementation, positioning you at the forefront of AMZL's AI transformation. You'll work at the intersection of operations, analytics, and automation, focusing on complex analytical problems that impact our intra-week forecasting and capacity management processes while developing and deploying AI-driven solutions that scale across our network.

You'll have the opportunity to architect and implement automated workflows, machine learning models, and intelligent decision-support systems that transform how we operate. Your dual expertise in business operations and technical execution will be essential as you translate operational challenges into automated solutions, leveraging AI/ML capabilities to drive efficiency at scale. Using advanced SQL, Python/R programming, and emerging AI technologies, you'll create intelligent dashboards, predictive models, and automated reporting systems that enable proactive decision-making and help avoid in-station volume risks.

You'll partner with cross-functional teams including Operations, Data Science, and Engineering to develop and deploy automation solutions that streamline processes and implement AI-powered improvements. This position requires someone who can speak both the language of business operations and technical implementation, bridging the gap between operational needs and technical possibilities.

This position offers exceptional growth opportunities as you develop expertise in AMZL systems, AI/ML applications, and automation frameworks. You'll learn to balance tactical execution with strategic thinking while contributing to the scaling of Central Operations through intelligent automation. Your ability to translate data into actionable insights and automated solutions will be crucial in driving continuous improvement in our last-mile delivery network.

The ideal candidate will combine strong technical and analytical skills with deep operational understanding, excellent communication abilities, and a passion for AI-driven process improvement. If you're excited about solving complex operational challenges through automation and contributing to the AI transformation of logistics operations, we want to hear from you.

Key job responsibilities

AI & Automation Development


Design and implement AI-powered automation solutions to streamline operational processes
Develop machine learning models for predictive analytics and intelligent forecasting
Build automated decision-support systems that scale across the network
Create intelligent workflows that reduce manual intervention and improve accuracy
Partner with Data Science teams to deploy AI/ML solutions into production
Evaluate and integrate emerging AI technologies to solve business challenges


Analysis & Process Improvement


Develop diagnostic analyses to identify automation opportunities and operational inefficiencies
Design and implement solutions that combine business logic with technical automation
Analyze complex data sets using advanced analytics and AI techniques to generate insights
Partner with stakeholders to translate business requirements into automated technical solutions
Optimize daily operational tasks through intelligent automation and process redesign
Monitor and analyze key performance metrics using predictive analytics to identify trends


Technical Execution & Implementation


Apply advanced SQL, Python/R programming to build scalable analytical solutions
Create and maintain intelligent dashboards with automated alerting and anomaly detection
Develop automated data pipelines and ETL processes for real-time insights
Build and document repeatable, automated analytical processes and workflows
Implement data quality monitoring systems with automated validation
Deploy code and automation solutions following software development best practices


Project Management & Strategic Thinking


Lead AI/automation projects from conception through deployment and scaling
Balance business requirements with technical feasibility in solution design
Design both tactical solutions and long-term strategic automation approaches
Provide technical and business input on prioritization and identify synergies between initiatives
Manage tradeoffs between speed, quality, scalability, and resources


Stakeholder Partnership & Knowledge Transfer


Partner with Operations, Engineering, Data Science, and technical teams
Translate complex technical concepts for business stakeholders and vice versa
Present AI/automation solutions and recommendations to leadership
Influence cross-functional teams to adopt automated solutions and meet shared goals
Mentor team members on both business operations and technical implementation
Build effective working relationships across business and technical functions


A day in the life

About The Team

Amazon Logistics, or AMZL, handles 'last mile' delivery duties in partnership with third-party distribution businesses. We utilize creative thinking, continuous improvement initiatives, and innovative AI/automation technologies to get millions of physical products into the hands of our customers. Our Central Operations team is at the forefront of AMZL's AI transformation, developing intelligent systems that revolutionize how we plan, execute, and optimize deliveries. Our goal is to make our customers' delivery experience as smooth as possible through automated, data-driven solutions that scale globally.

Basic Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification in Math, Engineering, Science or Business


Preferred Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or equivalent in a technical or analytical discipline
Experience developing AI/ML models or automated workflows in production environments
Experience with business intelligence and visualization tools (Tableau, QuickSight, Power BI, etc.)
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS) and modern data infrastructure (Redshift, S3, etc.)
Experience writing technical documentation, business requirements, and functional specifications
Background in process automation, workflow optimization, or intelligent systems
Experience working in agile/iterative development environments
Track record of successfully deploying technical solutions that solve business problems


Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

Title: Root Cause Analyst
Description:
Contract Type: Permanent, Full-time

Location: UK – Flexible/hybrid

At Domestic & General, we’re committed to delivering great customer outcomes, and when things don’t go as planned, we act fast to understand why and make it right.

About The Role

As a Root Cause Analyst, you’ll play a key role in uncovering the underlying drivers of poor customer outcomes and helping the business fix them for good. Using structured analysis and proven methodologies, you’ll identify trends, diagnose root causes, and work collaboratively with teams across the organisation to implement meaningful change.

This is an exciting opportunity to influence how we improve our processes, strengthen our controls, and enhance customer journeys across the business.

About You

To be successful in this role, you will be have:


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex data
Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal, with experience influencing stakeholders
Ability to manage multiple priorities and adapt in a fast-paced environment
High attention to detail and a structured, methodical approach
Experience working in a high-volume, customer-focused environment
Knowledge of financial services and regulatory expectations is highly desirable


The Role


Analyse customer outcome data from multiple sources to identify trends and areas of concern
Lead root cause investigations to identify underlying issues and contributing factors
Work with business stakeholders to design and implement solutions that reduce customer harm
Build and manage outcome improvement plans to track progress and drive accountability
Conduct deep-dive and thematic reviews to prioritise improvement opportunities
Continuously enhance reporting and MI to better support customer outcome analysis
Present insights and recommendations to senior stakeholders, influencing decision-making
Help embed a culture of continuous improvement and proactive issue resolution


Working for us, works for you


Personal and Professional growth is encouraged, with Internal Mobility programmes providing networking opportunities and Leadership development programmes.
Work-Life balance with a hybrid role / Health Cash Plan / Instant discounts / the new credit building tool / Salary Finance Loans / electric vehicle leasing / long service awards
Free Domestic & General protection plan – one free plan each year with access to discounted rates of up to 50% on additional plans, including referrals for family and friends


The culture at Domestic at General is creative, fast-moving and exciting as we’re going through a period of transformational change where customers are at the heart of everything we do… do not miss out on an opportunity to be part of both our culture and growth, apply now!

Domestic & General are an equal opportunities employer, which means we treat people fairly. We view all applications equally, regardless of gender, colour, ethnic background, religion, disability, age, sexual orientation, gender reassignment or marital/family status. We also have a thorough referencing process, which includes credit and criminal record checks.

At Domestic & General, we are proud of our 100-year legacy and excited about our future growth plans. We are expanding our horizons, entering new markets and territories internationally and we need your expertise to help us on the journey.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst – EIA/FES (DAP & Palantir Foundry)

Role Purpose

Support Energy Insights & Analysis (EIA) and Future Energy Scenarios (FES) by delivering high‑quality analysis, dashboards and data products on NESO’s Data & Analytics Platform (DAP) using Palantir Foundry. The role focuses on transforming complex energy system data into trusted insights that inform modelling, scenario development and strategic decision‑making.

Key Responsibilities


Perform exploratory and structured analysis on large, complex datasets (time‑series, scenario, market, network and policy data)
Support EIA/FES modelling teams by preparing, validating and analysing input and output datasets
Develop reproducible analysis workflows using SQL and Python within Foundry
Produce clear, well‑structured insight packs, visualisations and narratives for senior and non‑technical stakeholders
Work closely with Data Scientists, Data Engineers and Modellers to align analysis with business and policy questions
Ensure data outputs meet quality, governance, lineage and assurance requirements
Contribute to continuous improvement of analytical standards, templates and reusable assets across EIA/FES


Essential Skills & Experience


Strong experience in SQL and data analysis using Python
Experience analysing time‑series and multi‑source datasets
Strong data visualisation and storytelling skills
Ability to explain analytical findings clearly to non‑technical audiences


Desirable


Experience in energy systems, utilities, infrastructure or public‑sector analytics
Familiarity with scenario analysis, modelling outputs or forecasting concepts
Experience working in assurance‑led or regulated environments
Understanding of DAP and Foundry concepts (data products, lineage, governed analytics)


Ways of Working


Agile, collaborative delivery
Strong partnership with modelling, policy and digital teams
Focus on accuracy, transparency and reusability

Title: Data Analysis Manager
Description:
Role title: Data Analysis Manager




TPR grade & pay group: Grade 7 & Digital




Directorate: Digital, Data & Technology




Salary: £63,213.00 Per Annum




Contract type: Permanent




Location: Brighton: Hybrid (6 days per month in the office)




The Role And Responsibilities




We are looking for someone to ensure framework for measuring the success and performance of TPR continues to both drive and reflect the implementation of our Data Strategy and TPR’s wider objectives. The postholder will ensure TPR continues to demonstrate best practice in measuring and forecasting operational performance and relevant external landscape changes and enabling others to improve performance and set future priorities and approaches. This person will oversee the delivery of MI and analysis reporting at all levels and audiences, including TPR Board, ensuring it is as user centric as possible and supports the key discussions and decisions.




This role will work closely with stakeholders and delivery partners (including broader TPR and DWP) to use MI and analysis to build shared understanding of landscape changes, identify emerging risks and ensure these are used to inform operational, policy and strategy decisions.




This postholder will also be a function leader within TPR’s wider analytical community, they will work with function leaders from TPR’s other analytics teams, to ensure there is a strong and coherent analytics capability and architecture across TPR.




This role will involve the following responsibilities.




Role responsible for day-to-day delivery of analytics and performance services, ensuring quality and timeliness of delivery across business, with a focus on continuous improvement and innovation. This will include line management and leadership of a team of approx. 6-7 analysts.
Own and maintain the overall performance framework ensuring it reflects and drives progress towards TPR’s strategic priorities and objectives.
Accountable to Data Management Lead to deliver performance measurement, MI, modelling and forecasting required to underpin the good running and future planning of other functions in TPR.
The role manages meeting the analysis needs of internal and external stakeholders. Attend Operational Analytics Group, DWP Pensions Analytics/Insight forums and other data-analytics

Title: Smart Data Analyst
Description:
Closing Date

2026-07-02

Job Title: Smart Data Analyst

Location: Durrington, Worthing - Hybrid

Contract Type: Permanent

Hours: 37

Salary: Up to £37k based on skills and experience

About The Role

The Smart Metering Analyst is responsible for supporting the delivery of Southern Water’s smart metering programme through the provision of accurate, timely, and insightful performance and management information.

The role contributes to a key strategic initiative aimed at reducing water abstraction, minimising environmental and carbon impacts, and improving operational efficiency.

By enabling improved visibility of water usage and system performance, the analyst supports enhanced customer engagement, more effective leakage identification, and continuous improvement in customer experience.

What You Will Be Responsible For

Reporting to the Metering Assurance Lead, the Smart Metering Analyst works closely with the Smart Operations Centre (SOC) and the Alternative Metering Service (AMS) provider to analyse and interpret data across the end-to-end metering journey.

The role is responsible for collating data from multiple sources, assessing performance against key performance indicators (KPIs), targets, and regulatory requirements, and identifying trends, risks, and areas for improvement. A core aspect of the role involves producing clear and effective data visualisations and reports that provide stakeholders with meaningful insights into programme performance and support informed decision-making.

The Smart Metering Analyst is also accountable for ensuring that all reporting outputs meet internal standards and external regulatory requirements. This includes delivering performance reports within agreed service levels, supporting submissions to external bodies such as MOSL, Water UK, and Ofwat, and maintaining the accuracy and integrity of reported data.

Through ongoing analysis and continuous improvement of reporting processes, the role helps ensure that the smart metering programme operates efficiently, meets its objectives, and delivers measurable benefits to both the organisation and its customers.


Reporting: Deliver timely, fit‑for‑purpose performance reporting within agreed SLAs, continuously improving efficiency.
Data Analysis: Collate, analyse, and interpret data from multiple sources to report performance against KPIs, targets, and regulatory requirements.
Data Visualisation: Produce clear and effective visualisations to provide end‑to‑end visibility of programme performance.
Stakeholder Support: Provide accurate performance information to support decision‑making, including submissions to external stakeholders such as MOSL, WaterUK, and Ofwat.
Regulatory Reporting: Ensure accurate and timely data collation to meet regulatory reporting obligations.


What You’ll Bring To The Role


Knowledge of performance reporting and management information within complex programmes.
Understanding of data sources, data quality, and validation principles.
Knowledge of reporting and visualisation tools and how they support decision‑making.
Awareness of regulatory reporting requirements and compliance in a regulated environment.


Essential


Experience in data, analytics, business analysis, or a related field.
Experience in performance reporting or data analysis within a complex organisation.
Strong working knowledge of Excel and reporting/visualisation tools.


Southern Water is at the forefront of transforming Britain’s water industry, investing significantly to enhance resilience, sustainability, and service excellence. With £7.8bn planned investment for 2025-30, this is an unparalleled opportunity to join a business committed to delivering a generational shift in the way water services are managed.

You will be joining at a time of significant change, working alongside a highly skilled leadership team with a clear vision for the future. We offer an environment where senior professionals can make a meaningful impact, influence major strategic decisions, and drive long-term value creation

“Your potential matters more than a perfect checklist. If this role excites you and you believe you can make an impact, we want to hear from you! Skills can be learned—passion, drive, and fresh perspectives are what truly set candidates apart. Don’t hesitate—take the leap and apply!”

If this role isn’t quite what you’re looking for but are keen to be contacted about opportunities at Southern Water, you can register your details here: Introduce Yourself (myworkdaysite.com- Introduce Yourself)

Our Commitment to Diversity

We welcome applicants from all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability, religion, or any other protected characteristic. If you need reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Additional Information

In line with Southern Water’s security requirements, successful candidates will be required to provide evidence of their identity, eligibility to work in the UK, criminal record check (DBS) and verification of their employment and/or education history for the past three years.

Appointment to this role is subject to the successful completion of all pre‑employment checks, including security vetting.

Please note that if a candidate does not meet the required security standards or fails to pass the vetting process, Southern Water reserves the right to withdraw the offer of employment.

Some positions may also require higher levels of security vetting, which may involve providing additional documentation.

Title: Pricing Analyst
Description:
The Role

We’re looking for a curious, analytical and commercially minded Pricing Analyst to join our growing team. This is a broad, high‑impact role where you’ll work across a wide range of pricing projects, collaborating closely with underwriting, marketing and commercial teams to help drive Agria’s continued growth.




You’ll play a key part in shaping pricing decisions across both direct and partner channels - using data, market insight and modelling techniques to support profitable growth, strong customer outcomes and robust governance.




Working alongside colleagues across pricing, underwriting and the wider business, you’ll deliver analysis, monitor performance and contribute to the development of our pricing models and optimisation capabilities.




This role is offered on a 12 month Fixed Term Contract basis.




What You’ll Be Doing

Supporting pricing strategy - translating business priorities into clear analytical recommendations and pricing actions across direct and partnership channels.
Analysing performance - reviewing sales, conversion and retention trends to identify risks, opportunities and drivers of customer lifetime value.
Developing pricing models - contributing to the build, enhancement and monitoring of pricing and optimisation models using statistical, actuarial or data‑science techniques.
Assessing pricing impacts - evaluating proposed pricing and product changes to balance commercial performance, risk and customer outcomes.
Supporting governance - ensuring pricing changes are deployed accurately with full documentation, impact assessments and governance sign‑off.
Resolving pricing issues - investigating queries and errors, improving processes and reducing repeat demand.
Managing day‑to‑day queries - prioritising and responding to requests, escalating where needed.
Collaborating across teams - sharing expertise, supporting colleagues and contributing to a high‑performing, inclusive team culture.
Ensuring fair value - making sure all pricing activity supports good customer outcomes and meets internal governance standards.




What We’re Looking For

Insurance pricing expertise - strong understanding of general insurance pricing processes and methodologies; pet pricing experience is ideal.
Technical capability - experience with pricing software such as Quantee, Earnix or Radar.
Modelling skills - confident in GLMs and/or machine‑learning approaches to risk pricing.
Analytical strength - excellent numerical and statistical skills with the ability to interpret complex data and turn insights into actionable recommendations.
Data‑tool proficiency - comfortable using tools such as Excel, SAS, SQL, Python, R or specialist pricing platforms.
Commercial awareness - understanding how pricing decisions influence growth, retention, profitability and customer outcomes.
Customer‑focused mindset - committed to delivering fair value and strong customer experiences.
Collaborative approach - enthusiastic, proactive and supportive within a team environment.
Strong communication skills - able to explain analysis clearly to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
Organised & detail‑driven - able to manage competing priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
Curious & proactive - always looking for better ways of doing things.




Why choose Agria Pet Insurance:

Agria Pet Insurance is one of the UK's leading specialist pet insurance providers. We only provide lifetime insurance for animals, so truly understand and care about our customers and their beloved pets - especially as most of our team are pet parents, and we're all animal-lovers. Our passion for pets means that we are trusted by The Royal Kennel Club, Lloyds and many other significant affinity partners to provide lifetime pet insurance in their names.

Working closely with animal welfare organisations, vets and breeders, gives us deep insight on what's best for pets. And we give back too - including supporting hundreds of animal rescues, protecting over 100,000 rescued animals, and through donations of over £2m to animal charities.

We are a strong, growing business, having doubled our GWP in the last 5 years, with a supportive ethos and family feel. We became one of the UK’s Best Workplaces™ in 2023, 2024, 2025 and again in 2026, we have the top 4 slots on Which? Best Buy pet insurance, are ranked 4.8 on Trustpilot (higher than any other pet insurance provider) and we are extremely proud to be the only carbon negative pet insurer in the UK.




What we offer:

25 days annual leave which increases with service, plus bank holidays and an extra day off for your Birthday!
Opportunity to buy/sell up to 5 days annual leave per calendar year
Contributory pension and Life Assurance scheme
Access to 25% off insuring your pet with Agria. Friends and family are also entitled to a discount too
We aim to support the health and wellbeing of all our colleagues so you’ll have access to a Health Cash Plan, mental health support and health and wellbeing platforms, including Headspace
Free tickets to a huge variety of pet related events
Complimentary refreshments in our breakout area
Regular team activities and events arranged by our Employee Forum

Our passion for pets means that we are trusted by The Royal Kennel Club, Lloyds and many other significant affinity partners to provide lifetime pet insurance in their names.

Working closely with animal welfare organisations, vets and breeders, gives us deep insight on what's best for pets. And we give back too - including supporting hundreds of animal rescues, protecting over 100,000 rescued animals, and through donations of over £2m to animal charities.

We are a strong, growing business, having doubled our GWP in the last 5 years, with a supportive ethos and family feel. We became one of the UK’s Best Workplaces™ in 2023, 2024, 2025 and again in 2026, we have the top 4 slots on Which? Best Buy pet insurance, are ranked 4.8 on Trustpilot (higher than any other pet insurance provider) and we are extremely proud to be the only carbon negative pet insurer in the UK.


What we offer:

  • 25 days annual leave which increases with service, plus bank holidays and an extra day off for your Birthday!
  • Opportunity to buy/sell up to 5 days annual leave per calendar year
  • Contributory pension and Life Assurance scheme
  • Access to 25% off insuring your pet with Agria. Friends and family are also entitled to a discount too
  • We aim to support the health and wellbeing of all our colleagues so you’ll have access to a Health Cash Plan, mental health support and health and wellbeing platforms, including Headspace
  • Free tickets to a huge variety of pet related events
  • Complimentary refreshments in our breakout area
  • Regular team activities and events arranged by our Employee Forum

Title: Data and Insights Analyst [Internal Applicants Only]
Description:
Applications are invited for the role of Data and Insights Analyst. This opportunity is open to current University of Cambridge employees only.

The Role

The role focuses on managing and analysing institutional data to support decision-making and statutory reporting within the university. It involves ensuring data accuracy and reliability, developing business intelligence (BI) dashboards and reports, and delivering actionable insights to a variety of internal stakeholders. Further responsibilities include conducting quantitative analyses, developing forecasting models, improving data processes and systems, contributing to regulatory submissions, and responding to information requests, such as Freedom of Information (FOI) requests. The postholder also trains users on data tools, manages incoming queries, and presents analytical outputs to both internal and external audiences.

About You

Educated to first degree level, the ideal candidate will have experience in analysing complex data and working with large, multi-source datasets, together with a sound knowledge of statistical methods and their appropriate use. Experience of business intelligence and management information tools such as Tableau and Alteryx is important, and strong analytical skills, a high level of numeracy and the ability to explain complex numerical and financial information are essential. For a full person specification, key responsibilities and essential requirements, against which your application will be scored, please see the Further Particulars.

Benefits of working at Cambridge University include

Competitive rates of pay with automatic service-related pay progression and annual cost of living increases

Generous annual leave allowance of 41 days paid leave (including bank holidays)

Generous maternity, adoption and shared parental leave entitlement and other family friendly schemes (e.g. workplace nurseries and salary exchange schemes for childcare)

An auto-enrolment pension scheme, with a generous employer contribution

Travel benefits and retail discounts at over 2,000 local and national stores

The University of Cambridge is committed to supporting our staff to thrive both professionally and personally. We aim to support a good work/life balance, whilst retaining the positive aspects of our unique in-person environment and culture. Elements of this role are suitable for hybrid working, where staff can split their time between in-person collaboration and remote working. The frequency of the remote elements of the role can be discussed with the recruiting manager.

Click the 'Apply' button below to register an account with our recruitment system (if you have not already) and apply online.

Please upload your CV and cover letter to the application. Please note that additional uploaded documents will not be considered as part of the recruitment.

Informal enquiries about the role should be directed to: Mrs Ligia C Arias-Aldana, Senior Data and Insights Analyst (Data Management), Email: Constanza.Arias-Aldana@admin.cam.ac.uk

If you have any queries regarding the application process, please contact Karin Kuchta, HR Coordinator, Email: acdhr@admin.cam.ac.uk.

Please quote reference AK50069 on your application and in any correspondence about this vacancy.

The University actively supports equality, diversity and inclusion and encourages applications from all sections of society.

The University has a responsibility to ensure that all employees are eligible to live and work in the UK.

Title: Procurement & Purchasing Data Analyst
Description:
About PerkinElmer

Imagine a world where you could detect health issues sooner to treat them more effectively. Where food and water are always safe, even in remote corners of the earth. And where scientific and medical research are enhanced to solve the greatest challenges of our times. At PerkinElmer, we imagine this world every day. Then, we innovate and collaborate to make it happen everywhere.

Our dedicated team of about 16,000 employees worldwide, pioneers scientific technologies for better detection, imaging, and informatics to help our customers work to create healthier families, improve the quality of life, and sustain the well-being and longevity of people globally.

If you are seeking a meaningful, impactful, and stimulating career, look no further.

The Procurement & Purchasing Data Analyst will partner with Procurement, Finance and Insights to ensure customer and PerkinElmer data is complete, accurate, and accessible. This role has a specific focus on contract intelligence, vendor performance, and procurement decision support - translating operational data from OneSource and ServiceMax into actionable insights that drive smarter contracting decisions. The Data Analyst duties include collaborating with other data professionals to extract data figures, creating reports based on their findings, and monitoring key performance indicators (KPIs) to determine business initiatives’ success.

Job Responsibilities:


Interpret data, analyze results using statistical techniques and provide ongoing reports
Develop and implement databases, data collection systems, data analytics and other strategies that optimize statistical efficiency and quality
Acquire data from primary or secondary data sources and maintain databases/data systems
Identify, analyze, and interpret trends or patterns in complex data sets
Filter and “clean” data by reviewing computer reports, printouts, and performance indicators to locate and correct code problems
Work with management to prioritize business and information needs
Locate, define, and go live with new process improvement opportunities


Vendor Performance & Capability Insights


Provide visibility into vendor performance beyond individual assets - analyzing trends across asset classes, sites, and regions, including response times and resolution rates
Support identification of appropriate vendors when assets are not on contract, enabling comparisons based on capability, speed of response, and repeat visit rates


Define What Good Looks Like For Contract Recommendations


Specify and maintain mandatory elements for every contract recommendation, including: contract status and entitlement, warranty position, asset criticality and utilization, failure and downtime history, and strategic supplier linkage


Contract Data Ownership & Quality


Own the contract data model linking assets, contracts, entitlements, and work orders; identify and take accountability for data quality
Maintain system linkage between contract records and operational performance data, ensuring integrity across connected platforms


Contract Decision Rules


Translate operational practice into structured decision logic covering: non-negotiable rules (warranty, regulatory), best-practice guidance (risk-based coverage), prioritisation logic (criticality vs redundancy vs cost vs uptime), and retrospective review logic to assess whether the right contract decisions have historically been made - including ad hoc vs contract spend analysis


Basic Qualifications:


Technical expertise regarding data models, database design development, data mining and segmentation techniques
Strong knowledge of and experience with reporting packages (Business Objects etc), databases (SQL etc), programming (XML, Javascript, or ETL frameworks)
Knowledge of statistics and experience using statistical packages for analyzing datasets (Excel, SPSS, SAS etc)
Strong analytical skills with the ability to collect, organize, analyze, and disseminate significant amounts of information with attention to detail and accuracy
Adept at queries, report writing and presenting findings
Bachelors Degree and 5+ years of relevant work experience OR
Associates Degree and 7+ years of relevant work experience OR
Highschool Diploma and 10+ years of relevant work experience


Preferred Experience:


Proven working experience as a Data Analyst or Business Data Analyst
BS in Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Information Management or Statistics or related experience
Experience with Data Visualization Software, preferably PowerBI
Proficient in use of SAP or similar ERP systems
Knowledge of EAM / CMMS systems desired; experience with SAP
Understanding of procurement and contract management principles, including vendor assessment, entitlement management, and spend analysis
Experience querying or working with Salesforce / ServiceMax data, or equivalent CRM / FSM platforms, to support operational and procurement reporting
Excellent written and oral communication skills
Proven operational ability to visualize, identify steps and implement processes
Exhibits strong bias for action and results orientation, tackling tough problems with firm deadlines, championing positive change

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Job Description

Are you a seasoned Analytics Engineer, with knowledge of how to translate business needs into technical data requirements and the aptitude to do root-cause analysis to test and troubleshoot data solutions to support business users? You’ll need good coding experience in SQL/Python/R or any other pipeline language to produce high quality ETL/ELT flows and ideally some knowledge of data management and governance. We are hiring for an Analytics Engineer to build, test and deploy, advanced analytical models and visualisations to provide insights while producing scalable data products.

A great benefits package is offered in a culture where career development, with ongoing manager guidance, collaboration, flexibility, diversity and inclusivity are all intrinsic to our culture.  There is a huge investment in SHL currently so there’s no better time to become a part of something transformational.  Hybrid working is available.

What You Will Be Doing


Take ownership of building, testing and deploying advanced analytical models and visualisations to provide insights while producing scalable data products.
Work with internal stakeholders to assess, refine and prioritise analytics use cases and requirements and translate into technical model specifications, including: the pricing team, finance team and, commercial team.
Assess SHL’s existing data landscape and leverage it to develop semantic layers and reports and evolve our reporting landscape in line with our business strategy.
Collaborate with the Business Intelligence team to improve the data landscape and enable faster, more accurate insights through improved data products
Build robust end-to-end data products based on strategic semantic layers, balancing speed of delivery and the need for scalable and reliable data engineering
Promote optimum practices in data engineering and aid a culture of high-quality, reliable data across the organisation


Essential

What we are looking for from you:


Can translate business needs into technical data requirements and explain to users
SQL/Python/R or any other pipeline language to produce ETL/ELT flows
Root-cause analysis to test and troubleshoot data solutions using methodical approach
Good awareness of data management and governance


Desirable


Machine Learning and statistics
Experience in agile delivery


The candidate must have the right to work in the UK.

Get in touch: 

Find out how this one-off opportunity can help you to achieve your career goals by making an application to our knowledgeable Talent Acquisition team. Choose a new path with SHL.

We unlock the possibilities of businesses through the power of people, science and technology.
We started this industry of people insight more than 40 years ago and continue to lead the market with powerhouse product launches, ground-breaking science and business transformation.
When you inspire and transform people’s lives, you will experience the greatest business outcomes possible. SHL’s products insights, experiences, and services can help achieve growth at scale.

Title: Data Product Analyst - Digital Manufacturing
Description:
We are Reckitt

Home to the world's best loved and trusted hygiene, health, and nutrition brands. Our purpose defines why we exist: to protect, heal and nurture in the relentless pursuit of a cleaner, healthier world. We are a global team united by this purpose. Join us in our fight to make access to the highest quality hygiene, wellness, and nourishment a right and not a privilege.

Supply

Our supply chain is the backbone of our business. It's how we get our trusted products to people all over the world, safely and efficiently. And it's our talented and passionate teams that make this happen. If you're looking for a career in supply chain, there's no better place to be than Reckitt. We offer a variety of exciting opportunities in all areas of the supply chain, from planning and procurement to manufacturing and logistics. You could join our Global Supply Planning team to develop and implement our global supply strategy, or work with our Procurement Centre of Excellence team to negotiate and manage our supplier relationships. Our Manufacturing Excellence team help us improve the efficiency, quality and safety of our manufacturing operations, and our Logistics Excellence team develop new and innovative ways to distribute our products to customers.

About The Role

We are looking for a Data Product Analyst to strengthen our Digital Manufacturing team and ensure that our digital solutions are built on a coherent, scalable and connected data foundation. This role sits at the intersection of manufacturing, data, and digital platforms, with a strong focus on connecting systems, defining data structures, and enabling data‑driven decision-making across our global factory network. The role is critical in ensuring that Digital Manufacturing initiatives translate into consistent, reliable, and scalable solutions — enabling better operational decisions at site level.

Your responsibilities


Data Integration & System Understanding
Map and understand end‑to‑end data flows across: shopfloor systems (PLCs, SCADA), MES and operational systems, ERP (e.g. SAP), data platforms (e.g. Siemens on AWS)
Define and document: master systems vs. slave systems, data ownership and responsibilities, points of inconsistency or redundancy
Data Architecture & Platform Enablement
Support the design of practical, scalable data architecture
Ensure alignment between: operational systems, cloud platforms, analytics environments
Work closely with IT to ensure that solutions are: technically robust, aligned with business needs
Connectivity & Data Standards
Define and enforce: data connectivity standards, data ingestion logic, naming conventions and structuring principles
Ensure consistency across sites and use cases
Support integration into the Siemens data platform (AWS) environment
Support Digital Use Case Delivery
Translate business use cases (e.g. performance management, predictive maintenance) into: data requirements, data models, system dependencies
Work closely with: Digital Product Owners, site teams, IT&D
Transparency & Governance Support
Provide visibility on: data dependencies, readiness and gaps, risks related to data availability
Support the PMO with: structured inputs, data-related decision support
Help ensure a single, consistent data logic across the programme


The experience we're looking for


2–5 years experience in:
manufacturing / CPG environment
data analytics / data engineering / industrial data
Exposure to:
supply chain processes (basic understanding)
digital or IT-enabled manufacturing environments
Experience with data platforms (e.g. Siemens, Azure, AWS) is a plus


The skills for success


Degree in: Engineering, Data Science, Industrial IT or a related field
Basic to solid proficiency in:
SQL, Python (or similar)
data modelling
data pipelines / APIs
Understanding of:
cloud environments (AWS desirable)
industrial systems (MES / SCADA / PLC concepts)
System thinking: ability to connect data across systems and processes
Structured approach: translates complexity into clear, usable models
Pragmatism: focuses on practical solutions, not theory
Communication: able to interact with:
factory teams
IT architects
business stakeholders
Curiosity & ownership mindset: strong drive to understand “how things really work”


What we offer

With inclusion at the heart of everything we do, working alongside our four global Employee Resource Groups, we support our people at every step of their career journey, helping them to succeed in their own individual way. We invest in the wellbeing of our people through parental benefits, an Employee Assistance Program to promote mental health, and life insurance for all employees globally. We have a range of other benefits in line with the local market. Through our global share plans we offer the opportunity to save and share in Reckitt's potential future successes. For eligible roles, we also offer short-term incentives to recognise, appreciate and reward your work for delivering outstanding results. You will be rewarded in line with Reckitt's pay for performance philosophy.

Equality

We recognise that in real life, great people don't always 'tick all the boxes'. That's why we hire for potential as well as experience. Even if you don't meet every point on the job description, if this role and our company feels like a good fit for you, we still want to hear from you. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, disability or medical condition; colour, ethnicity, race, citizenship, and national origin; religion, faith; pregnancy, family status and caring responsibilities; sexual orientation; sex, gender identity, gender expression, and transgender identity; protected veteran status; size or any other basis protected by appropriate law.

We recognise that in real life, great people don't always 'tick all the boxes'. That's why we hire for potential as well as experience. Even if you don't meet every point on the job description, if this role and our company feels like a good fit for you, we still want to hear from you. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, disability or medical condition; colour, ethnicity, race, citizenship, and national origin; religion, faith; pregnancy, family status and caring responsibilities; sexual orientation; sex, gender identity, gender expression, and transgender identity; protected veteran status; size or any other basis protected by appropriate law.

Title: R&D Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: R&D Data Analyst

Business Function: R&D

Location: Leeds Office and R&D

Job Purpose

The Deo R&D Data Analyst will play a key role in supporting the digital transformation journey of Deodorants R&D. The role holder will support the Data & Digital manager to drive engagement in a Digital first Data Culture through driving adoption of key tools & systems; as well as supporting the functional experts with analytics to aid data-led decision making. We are looking for people with a natural curiosity and desire to interrogate and connect data to help us translate R&D product and business data into insights that our R&D teams can use to develop more successful products.

Responsibilities


Support the Deo R&D Data & Digital Manager to drive adoption of key tools & systems which enable data capture for a digital transformation programme.
Identify, develop & implement priority analytics and data visualisation tools (together with functional experts, partners and stakeholders) using our suite of apps (e.g. PowerApps, PowerBI, Datalab) to enable confidence in rapid data driven R&D decisions across product development functions.
Support the Deo R&D Data & Digital Manager direct delivery of digital training (Data Literacy & Data Analytics Tool Awareness & adoption) across Deo R&D teams.
Proactively support the generation of key business/Deo R&D insights through data analytics and the visualisation of complex data sets across product development functions.
Identify and communicate examples of business impact from data analytics practices to further drive operational adoption of digital tools & applications across Deo R&D.
Support the Deo R&D Data & Digital Manager with the execution and monitoring of Data Quality to ensure our data is available for various business, analytical & modelling needs..


All About You

Education: GCSE English and Maths or equivalent, Degree level Scientist/Data Science

Relevant experience


Relevant experience in Digital technology development / application in industry.
Highly competent with data capture and analytics tools, to bring together large volumes of data from multiple sources (and teams)
Experience of applying statistical Design of Experiments approaches
Experience in data reporting, analysis/visualisation & modelling
Experience of working across multi-disciplinary teams
Knowledge of data management, analytics and data science coupled with an understanding of how to support, use and exploit these capabilities.
Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to make data & digital accessible for all.
Strong problem solving skills and the ability to work both independently and as part of a team
Strong data management skills – you understand the importance of data capture & governance
Confidence in manipulating/visualising large volumes of data from multiple sources with applications such as Excel, PowerBI, Access, Power App…
Have coding /modelling experience in any of Python, SAS, R, MATLAB, JMP.


NOTES

What We Offer

Not only do we offer a competitive salary and pension scheme, we also offer an annual bonus, subsidised gym membership, a discounted staff shop and shares. You’ll have the opportunity to work directly with our renowned and exciting brands in a flexible and hybrid working environment.

Whilst the role is advertised on a full-time basis, we would be happy to discuss possible flexible working options and what this may look like for you. We are a key advocate of wellbeing and offer a variety of support for our people including hubs, programmes and development opportunities. We strive to achieve a family-friendly and inclusive workplace and to, above all, create possibilities for all.

Diversity at Unilever is about inclusion, embracing differences, creating possibilities and growing together for better business performance. We embrace diversity in our workforce. This means giving full and fair consideration to all applicants and continuing development of all employees regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, sex, sexual orientation, marriage and civil partnership, and pregnancy and maternity. We are also more than happy to provide reasonable adjustments during our application and interview process to enable you to be present your best self. To find out more, including about our Employee Resource Groups, please click here Equity, Diversity & Inclusion at Unilever | Unilever.

Recruitment Fraud

Cyber criminals advertise fake job adverts with prestigious employers as a way of stealing information or even defrauding individuals out of money. In the most sophisticated cases, they will set up fake websites, which have a similar address to companies like Unilever. They even conduct fake telephone interviews and then offer candidates a role with the proviso they pay a fee for background checks or to cover work visa costs.  These types of attacks are becoming more common as more people are looking for employment in the economic climate.

How is Unilever tackling this?

Many of Unilever’s recruitment sites publish a warning to candidates about recruitment fraud. The Cyber Security team also proactively scan for signs of people setting up fake Unilever sites and act to close them down.

What can I do?

If you become aware of potential recruitment fraud, spot fake Unilever recruitment adverts or fake LinkedIn profiles, report them via Una Live Chat.

Unilever does not accept responsibility or liability for any candidates who are financially impacted by recruitment fraud. Your vigilance is key!

Title: Data Insights Lead
Description:
DATA INSIGHTS LEAD | LANGLEY, UK




Travel obsessed? Big tech fan? Hey, you’re in good company. If you want to be part of the industry that makes the world go round, then look no further.




Travelport is the brains behind lots of your travel bookings- plane, car or hotel. Our technology is used to book that magical holiday, long awaited bucket-list trip or overdue school reunion. While we can’t solve mosquito bites or lost luggage, we can simplify a lot of the technical parts of travel, and we’re looking for the best thinkers to help us do it.




We’re hiring right now for a Data Insights Lead! Search for #Travelport on LinkedIn and hear from our amazing team.




How you’ll make an impact:




The Team Lead, Data Insights Analysis provides hands‑on analytical leadership while guiding an early‑career analyst with strong potential. This role blends technical depth, people development, and consultative partnership across the People function. The leader ensures that the right teams deliver reporting and insights, in the most automated and scalable way, and that data is used effectively to support performance management, recognition, learning, and broader talent initiatives. A strong partnership with the Corporate Systems group—who own Oracle and other core HR platforms—is essential.




Your role in action:

Lead, coach, and develop one early‑career team member, providing structure, clarity, and growth opportunities while ensuring consistent delivery and accountability.
Serve as a consultative partner to People function teams on Core HR, performance management, recognition, learning, and other talent processes, helping them clarify needs, understand data usage, and anticipate future reporting requirements.
Partner closely with the Corporate Systems group to align data sources, system changes, and automation opportunities across Oracle and related HR tools.
Build Power BI dashboards, Reports Vizualization and measures from pre-built data models.
Ensure reports delivered by the appropriate teams and that end users receive insights in the most automated, scalable, and user‑friendly format.
Maintain data quality, documentation, and consistency across all outputs.
Prioritize incoming requests and manage team workload.
Identify opportunities to streamline processes, improve data flows, and enhance reporting capabilities across the People function.




Could this be you?

Experience in HR analytics, people reporting, or a related HR function role with significant exposure to data and reporting.
Strong understanding of core HR domains including performance management, compensation, learning & development, talent acquisition, and recognition.
Demonstrated ability to gather, document, and validate business and reporting requirements from non-technical stakeholders.
Experience coordinating or leading user acceptance testing, including writing test cases, managing feedback cycles, and obtaining sign-off.
Familiarity with enterprise HR platforms (such as Oracle HCM) and strong understanding of HR data structure and maintained within these systems.
Ability to translate business questions into analytical approaches and build visualizations in Microsoft Power BI, create measures using DAX on pre-built datasets.
Demonstrated experience mentoring, coaching, or leading others.
Strong organizational skills and comfort managing competing priorities.
Ability to understand upstream and downstream data impacts and advocate for data integrity across the People function.
Familiarity with building Oracle OTBI reports
Experience building automated workflows or integrations.
Knowledge of SQL queries on SQL Server and Reporting data warehouse concepts like star schema and normalizations etc.




Discover why our teams love working here:

Where do we start! Our benefits package includes:

We offer a package that includes 25 days annual leave per annum, hybrid working model, this role is bonus eligible, and bonus awards are performance based and governed by the applicable annual bonus plan terms, pension contribution, private medical, life assurance and personal travel insurance.




Search for #lifeattravelport & #travelportUK for our employee stories on LinkedIn!




Your journey at Travelport starts here.

Our application process is quick, easy, and hassle-free — apply in just a few minutes!

We are an equal opportunities employer and will consider all qualified applicants purely on their skills and abilities. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation, if needed.

Title: UK Analytics & Operations Specialist
Description:
UK Analytics & Operations Specialist - Opella

Reading UK

You’ve built experience, now use it where bold ideas meet real impact. At Opella, we’re challenging how self-care works for millions of people and need leaders and experts ready to shape what’s next. As an UK Analytics & Operations Specialist, you’ll bring your skill, your curiosity and your drive to make health simpler, smarter and more human.

About The Job

At Opella, we’re on a mission to make self-care as simple as it should be. As a UK Commercial Operations & Trade & Revenue Management (TRM) Analyst, you will work closely with the UK Country Head and TRM Lead to help execute the UK commercial strategy.

This is a high-visibility, developmental role combining commercial analytics, operational execution, and stakeholder engagement. Designed as a talent pipeline opportunity, it offers hands-on exposure across Trade & Revenue Management and business operations, equipping you with the skills and experience for future growth within Opella.

You will play a key role in enabling data-driven decisions, driving operational excellence, and strengthening collaboration across teams.

Key Responsibilities


Support the execution of TRM strategy, including pricing, portfolio optimisation, and channel mix
Contribute to Integrated Business Planning (IBP), including forecasting, performance tracking, and variance analysis
Maintain and optimise key commercial datasets and tools (e.g. pricing files, trade spend, forecasts)
Generate insights and analysis to support commercial performance and decision-making
Manage operational processes including purchase orders, invoicing, and supplier coordination
Coordinate internal activities, including team events, communications, and stakeholder materials
Lead or support continuous improvement initiatives, including automation and adoption of new tools


Key Requirements


Advanced Excel skills (Pivot Tables, Power Query; VBA/Macros a plus)
Strong analytical experience within commercial, financial, or operational environments
Excellent organisation and prioritisation skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks
Strong communication skills, with confidence presenting insights to senior stakeholders
Proactive, adaptable mindset with a willingness to challenge and improve existing processes
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, matrixed, and cross-functional environment
Collaborative approach with the ability to build relationships across teams (sales, finance, supply, marketing)


What Makes You Stand Out


Experience with coding or automation tools (e.g. Python)
Ability to translate complex data into clear, compelling business insights
High learning agility and strong ownership mindset
High energy, multi-tsking, office and stakeholder management


Why join Opella?

At Opella, we believe in creating an inclusive and empowering workplace where everyone can thrive. You’ll be part of a collaborative, forward-thinking team where your ideas are valued, your development is supported, and your impact is real.

We are committed to building a diverse workforce and welcome applications from all backgrounds.

About Us

Opella is the self-care challenger with the purest and third-largest portfolio in the Over The Counter (OTC) & Vitamins, Minerals & Supplements (VMS) market globally.

Our mission is to bring health in people’s hands by making self-care as simple as it should be. For half a billion consumers worldwide – and counting.

At the core of this mission is our 100 loved brands, our 11,000-strong global team, our 13 best-in-class manufacturing sites and 4 specialized science and innovation development centers. Headquartered in France, Opella is the proud maker of many of the world’s most loved brands, including Allegra, Buscopan, Doliprane, Dulcolax, Enterogermina, Essentiale and Mucosolvan.

As a globally certified B Corp company, we are active players in the journey towards healthier people and planet. Find out more about our mission at www.opella.com.

Title: Excel Reporting Analyst
Description:
Job Description

This role will support the London-based Global Client Development team by ensuring client requests are handled promptly, accurately, and to a high standard. The position will be pivotal in establishing the Lloyd’s Centre of Excellence as the primary internal resource for best practices related to the complexities of Lloyd’s market structures. Key responsibilities include completing and maintaining Claims Manager Programme setups, as well as coordinating management information updates in alignment with Lloyd’s requirements. The role will also provide support to our global operations to ensure processes are well-documented, consistently applied, and continuously improved to deliver a high-quality and reliable service.

Responsibilities


Ensure London Market client month end reporting requirements are met.
Complete Annual Due Diligence returns as required for our services across the world.
Support the relationship lead with contract co-ordination using our inhouse software LEAH.
Assist with successful onboarding of new programmes within claims manager and ensure renewals are completed resulting in a seamless experience for our claim handlers.
Issue fee renewal reminders to the relationship lead at defined intervals.
Identify and implement data quality checks removing the need for manual intervention at month end.
Centralised MI co-ordination e.g. when the market changes, all Lloyd’s reports are updated. There is one single source of the truth for Lloyd’s related programmes.
Complete performance analysis to inform growth conversations led by the relationship lead.
Troubleshoot system errors that stop our handlers from progressing the claim.
Monitor SLA performance on Lloyd’s programmes and identify areas of focus.
To be responsible for the upkeep of centrally held Lloyd’s related info and shared with teams around the world.


Qualifications


Advanced user of MS Office Suite (Excel, PowerPoint, etc.).
Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.).
Advanced Excel (vlookups, pivot tables, sorting, 'if' statements, concatenation).
Experience of using Claims Manager is a bonus.
Ability to perform open-ended investigation of data.
Ability to present results and coordinate solutions with a variety of parties.
Attention to detail when updating and maintaining centralized records.
Outstanding communication skills and ability to communicate effectively.
Excellent organizational, analytical, project management and client relation skills are essential.
Must be a detail oriented, analytical and an independent thinker.
Excellent written and spoken English.
Experience working with and presenting to a variety of parties is especially relevant.
Experience of Lloyd’s bordereau reporting is of benefit.


Understanding of the UK insurance market is preferable, specifically Lloyd’s

Title: Lead Data Analyst
Description:
Lead Data Analyst – Research & Insights | EdTech | £50,000 – £60,000

Hybrid (Bedford) - 2 days onsite per week

4-day work week available (pro-rated)




Join a well-established, award-winning EdTech company building engaging, maths-based learning products used by millions of children worldwide.




This is a hands-on role analysing product, user, and business data to generate insights that shape decision-making. You’ll uncover how learners engage with digital platforms, identify behavioural patterns, and influence product development and user experience — while also contributing to research that improves how children learn.




The Role

This is a high-impact Lead Data Analyst role combining product analytics with a strong research mindset.

You will lead a small and growing insights function (with one Research Associate), ensuring that data, analysis, and interpretation are robust, scalable, and drive real impact.




You will:

Analyse how users engage with learning platforms (behaviour, progress, engagement)
Identify patterns, trends, and opportunities for improvement
Evaluate the impact and effectiveness of product features and releases
Combine quantitative data with qualitative insights (e.g. user feedback)
Support research initiatives exploring learning outcomes
Work closely with leadership to define key analytical questions
Support the onboarding, coordination, and line management of a Research Associate
Work with tools such as Python, Looker, or similar




What We’re Looking For

4–6 years’ experience as a Data Analyst, Product Analyst, or similar
Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
Experience within EdTech or Education data analytics
Experience using Python or R for data analysis
Experience supervising or managing small teams (1–2 people)
Solid understanding of cohort analysis, segmentation, and statistical methods (e.g. regression, correlation)
Ability to critically evaluate data, including bias and confounding factors
Strong communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
A natural curiosity — particularly around learning and behaviour




Salary & Benefits

£50,000 – £60,000 (full-time equivalent)
Option to work a 4-day week (pro rata £40,000 – £48,000)
Hybrid working from Bedford - 2-3 days onsite
Pension
Private healthcare

You’ll uncover how learners engage with digital platforms, identify behavioural patterns, and influence product development and user experience — while also contributing to research that improves how children learn.


The Role

This is a high-impact Lead Data Analyst role combining product analytics with a strong research mindset.

You will lead a small and growing insights function (with one Research Associate), ensuring that data, analysis, and interpretation are robust, scalable, and drive real impact.


You will:

  • Analyse how users engage with learning platforms (behaviour, progress, engagement)
  • Identify patterns, trends, and opportunities for improvement
  • Evaluate the impact and effectiveness of product features and releases
  • Combine quantitative data with qualitative insights (e.g. user feedback)
  • Support research initiatives exploring learning outcomes
  • Work closely with leadership to define key analytical questions
  • Support the onboarding, coordination, and line management of a Research Associate
  • Work with tools such as Python, Looker, or similar


What We’re Looking For

  • 4–6 years’ experience as a Data Analyst, Product Analyst, or similar
  • Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets
  • Experience within EdTech or Education data analytics
  • Experience using Python or R for data analysis
  • Experience supervising or managing small teams (1–2 people)
  • Solid understanding of cohort analysis, segmentation, and statistical methods (e.g. regression, correlation)
  • Ability to critically evaluate data, including bias and confounding factors
  • Strong communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders
  • A natural curiosity — particularly around learning and behaviour


Salary & Benefits

  • £50,000 – £60,000 (full-time equivalent)
  • Option to work a 4-day week (pro rata £40,000 – £48,000)
  • Hybrid working from Bedford - 2-3 days onsite
  • Pension
  • Private healthcare

Title: Quality Assurance Data Analyst
Description:
Overview

Here at Mamas & Papas our beautifully designed nursery products & how we bring them to life with care, insight, quality, style & attention to detail is what makes us the brand of choice, & the brand of trust for an incredible community of new & expectant parents.

There is therefore no surprise that we’re continual winners at the Mumsnet Awards, Dadsnet Awards, Mother & Baby Awards, British Nursery Brand Awards, Which Best Buy, & so on, which make us, & all involved, so very proud to be part of.

Through continued growth, we are looking to strength this commitment to the quality & safety of what we create, by recruiting a QUALITY ASSURANCE DATA ANALYST.

The role of QA DATA ANAYST

This is a key role in delving deep into the data from our Spares & Repairs teams, as well as Customer Care, where our customers may have had an issue with a product. From the trends and data you gather you then work collaboratively with product designers, engineers, product testers as well as warehouse, distribution, stock control, and a multitude of other departments including finance, Buying etc to indentify commercial impacts, quality improvements and product performance.

Adding to this looking into environment reporting & data submissions.

This role being ideal for someone who loves delving into the mysteries of a good spreadsheet or two. Someone who has an inquisitive mind that can translate data into reality for teams to understand and take action upon.

There are not too many Data Analyst roles where the work that you do not only has an impact on an organisation, but a massive impact on a incredible community of new parents and their babies. You can really make a difference.

To apply for this QA Data Analyst opportunity, simply take those amazing first steps and APPLY TODAY, for a member of the recruitment team to give you a call to chat through this role further.

We at Mamas & Papas are committed to championing people of all cultures, ethnicities and gender preferences, and in celebrating diversity in all its forms through our campaigns, recruitment and our actions. We strongly believe in the power of inclusivity to help us all move forward in life.

Benefits

We're a business that cares. Here's just some of our benefits on offer;


33 days holiday, increasing up to 40 with service.
Buy & Sell holiday schemes
Company Bonus Schemes
Employer pension contribution from day 1 enrolment.
Significant staff discounts for family & friends from our shops & online
Ongoing offers & discounts across a variety of external organisations, from holidays, travel, restaurants, gifts & services to many more worthy discounts through our Benefits Hub
Subsidised health & critical illness cover and insurances
Supportive family related leave policies (including company enhanced pay for maternity, IVF & Fertility, surrogacy, paternity, adoption, shared parental leave)
Supportive Foster Care & Carers Leave offering
Support for Loss and Bereavement
A Menopause Friendly Employer
Employee Recognition and Appreciation Schemes
At Mamas & Papas, we’re a business for the future. Every day, we help to welcome a new generation of children into the world, and we want them to grow up in a world that’s sustainable – one that’s full of opportunity and hope.
Oh, and a great bunch of people to work with too.


We also operate at Head Office:


Hybrid flexi working of 3 days in the office.
Flexi-time schemes based around core working hours whether in the office, or working from home
Plus, free car parking, Prayer & Contemplation room, subsidised canteen, free tea & coffee, Games & TV rooms & much more.


(NB*: All benefits subject to individual schemes criteria & terms)

Title: Demand Analyst UK
Description:
Who we are:

We are Fyffes, one of the world's leading tropical produce companies. For more than 130 years, we have sourced, shipped and delivered high-quality fresh produce globally. With over 4,000 employees and a strong international network of growers and suppliers, we are committed to shaping wellbeing for the world through sustainable growth, innovation and strong partnerships.

Now we're looking for a Demand Analyst UK to join our team, turning UK Citrus and Melon demand into accurate forecasts, insight and planning support.




Here's what you will do:

Support the commercial team by analysing melon demand across the DACH region and translating orders, forecast trends, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market dynamics into actionable insight. Your work will support weekly and seasonal planning, availability, service levels and waste reduction.

You will connect demand insight with sourcing, logistics and supply chain teams, providing early risk visibility and structured performance reporting. The role is analytical, fast-paced and focused on improving forecast accuracy, demand visibility and planning discipline.




Some key responsibilities:

Analyse DACH melon demand using orders, historical sales, promotions, seasonality, weather impacts and market trends.
Prepare and maintain accurate weekly and seasonal melon demand forecasts with documented assumptions.
Track forecast versus actual demand and order movements, identifying variances, trends and root causes.
Produce demand, forecast, order and service reporting, including forecast accuracy, bias, fill rate, availability and waste.
Support demand reviews, planning inputs and internal alignment before market meetings and planning calls.
Identify supply-demand gaps, shortfalls, oversupply, timing risks and changes in demand early.
Support scenario analysis, mitigation planning, allocation discussions and continuous improvement in reporting and forecasting processes.




What you need to bring:

Degree in Supply Chain, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Agriculture or a related field.
1-3 years of experience in demand analysis, demand planning, supply chain, commercial analysis, fresh produce or FMCG.
Experience with retail demand, forecasts, orders, promotions or seasonal planning is advantageous.
Fresh produce, short shelf-life supply chain or melon experience is beneficial.
Strong Excel and data analysis capability, with ability to translate large datasets into clear insight.
Fluent English; Spanish or another language is advantageous.
Strong attention to detail, proactive communication, structured problem-solving, ownership and ability to work in a fast-paced perishable product environment.




What it is like to work at Fyffes:

We are ambitious but grounded, combining high performance with a people-first culture. Our leadership principles guide us every day: we play to win as one Fyffes, shape the future, cultivate a growth mindset, are transparent and inclusive, and are moved by doing good. We thrive on diversity and treat all candidates equally.




Interested? We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Post Title: Data Analyst

SBU/Department: Marketing and Communications

FTE: 1.0 FTE (working 37 hours per week)

Duration of Contract: Permanent

Salary: UH7 £38,784pa to £46,049pa by annual increments on achieving designated skills and experience

Annual Leave: 30 days plus standard public holidays and an additional 4 days, including the closure of our office between Christmas and New Year

Location: Hybrid – De Havilland Campus, MacLaurin Building, and at home




The University of Hertfordshire is looking for a skilled, motivated and proactive Data Analyst to join our Insight Team to support the University in transforming lives and achieving its strategic goals.




Main duties and responsibilities

As a member of the Insight team, your key responsibility will not only be to report on existing trends and ongoing recruitment performance but to identify and evaluate opportunities. To achieve this, you would be expected to be able to interpret and convert large complex data sets, using a range of methods and data transformation tools such as Tableau Prep or Microsoft Excel, into appropriate formatting for data visualisation. Most commonly, this would involve analysing data published by UCAS, HESA or our own admissions data.




You will also be asked to create forecasts and models on university applications and acceptances, and summarise and present comprehensive analytical findings in a variety of formats (including written reports), targeting different audiences to help convey key messages and inform Departmental or University policy and decision-making. The Data Analyst would additionally undertake bespoke research and analyses in response to requests from the department.




Skill and experience required

You will have experience working within a similar market intelligence-focused insight/research/ analytical role and demonstrable experience of constructively challenging assumptions and traditional views, identifying issues and generating/implementing new approaches, and adapting the tried and tested approaches to develop solutions to challenges and opportunities. Demonstrable experience of using specialist analytical and reporting software or advanced functionality of Microsoft Excel, employing a range of practices to analyse and explain complex data sets and demonstrable experience of using data visualisation software, e.g. Tableau, Qlikview and Power BI to help uncover the stories behind the data is essential.




Qualifications required

You will be educated with a minimum of a degree or equivalent in a numerical subject such as Mathematics, Physics, Computer Science or similar or equivalent proven experience.




Please view the job description and person specification for a full list of the duties and essential criteria. Please attach a personal statement showing clearly how your skills and experience match the Person Specification.




Internal applicants – please ensure you apply via your employee self-service portal.




An appointment to this role may require an Academic Technology Approval Scheme (ATAS) certificate.




We regret that we are unable to offer UKVI sponsorship for this post; applicants will either hold UK Right to Work or demonstrate access to UK Right to Work before an offer is made.




Contact Details/Informal Enquiries: Andre Grant, Head of Insight - a.grant3@herts.ac.uk




Closing Date: 05 July 2026

Interview Date: TBC

Reference Number: REQ000770




Our vision is to transform lives: UH is committed to Equality, Diversity and Inclusion and building a diverse community. We welcome applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of their protected characteristics and recognise that there are different ways applicants may achieve the criteria in this document. We offer a range of employee benefits, including generous annual leave, flexible location opportunities within the UK, discounted Sports Village memberships, free Active Staff sessions, personal and professional development and family-friendly policies. #GoHerts
