Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Our client is looking for a Data Analyst for a 3 months contract, 4 days on site in Markham. If you have the below requirements, please apply today!




We are seeking a detail-oriented and driven Data/Product Analyst to support the performance, insights, and evolution of our Water Portfolio, which includes rental water heaters, water purification solutions, and related offerings. This role will play a critical part in enabling data-driven decision making, optimizing product performance, and supporting strategic initiatives across the product lifecycle. The ideal candidate is highly analytical, comfortable working with large datasets, and passionate about uncovering insights that drive business outcomes. Experience in durable goods, home services, or recurring revenue models is a strong asset.




Key Responsibilities




Analytics & Insights

• Analyze product performance across key metrics such as customer acquisition, retention, pricing, and profitability • Leverage dashboards and reports to monitor trends and identify opportunities for improvement

• Conduct customer and market analysis to support roadmap development and strategic planning




Product Lifecycle Support

• Assist in managing product continuity, including unit additions and attrition analysis

• Support new product introductions with data-driven forecasts and performance tracking

• Collaborate with product managers to evaluate pricing strategies and promotional effectiveness




Financial & Business Support

• Contribute to business case development for new initiatives and product enhancements

• Partner with Finance and Supply Chain to support forecasting, cost analysis, and margin optimization

• Help track and report on category-level P&L performance




Cross-functional Collaboration

• Work closely with Sales, Marketing, Engineering, and Field Operations to support go-to market execution and service readiness

• Participate in cross-category projects to ensure alignment and synergy across the broader product team




Qualifications

• 2+ years of experience in product analysis, business analytics, or a related role

• Proficiency in Excel, SQL, and data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI)

• Solid understanding of financial concepts and experience supporting P&L or business case development

• Excellent communication and collaboration skills

• Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, or a related field

Title: Data & Reporting Analyst - SQL (Power BI and Tableau)
Company: Astra-North Infoteck Inc. ~ Conquering today’s challenges, achieving tomorrow’s vision!
Description:
Data & Reporting Analyst


Location: Toronto
Work Model: Hybrid (4 Days WFO)
Duration: 12 Months
Top 3 Required Skills


SQL

Power BI

Tableau


Job Description


We are seeking a skilled Data & Reporting Analyst to join the analytics team and support data-driven decision-making across the organization. The ideal candidate will have strong SQL expertise, hands-on experience with reporting tools such as Tableau and Power BI, and the ability to build scalable reporting and analytics solutions.


The candidate will work closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and leadership teams to deliver actionable insights, optimize reporting processes, and improve data accessibility across the enterprise.


Key Responsibilities


SQL Development & Data Analysis


Write complex and optimized SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple sources.

Improve query performance and ensure efficient data retrieval.

Convert repetitive manual queries into reusable stored procedures, functions, and views.


Reporting & Dashboard Development


Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Tableau and Power BI.

Build scalable and user-friendly reporting solutions for business stakeholders.

Support self-service analytics initiatives through structured reporting frameworks.


Semantic Layer & Data Modeling


Design semantic layers and logical data models to support consistent reporting and analytics.

Ensure data consistency, integrity, and usability across reporting assets.


Database Migration & Optimization


Support migration of stored procedures and reporting logic across database platforms.

Validate data accuracy and minimize disruptions during migration activities.


Stakeholder Collaboration


Gather business requirements and translate them into analytical and reporting solutions.

Partner with cross-functional teams including business users, leadership, and engineering teams.


Data Quality & Governance


Maintain data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across dashboards and reporting environments.

Support data governance and reporting standards.


Required Qualifications


Strong hands-on experience with advanced SQL and query optimization.

Experience building dashboards and reports using Tableau and/or Power BI.

Good understanding of semantic layer design and data modeling concepts.

Experience with stored procedures, functions, and reusable SQL frameworks.

Knowledge of relational databases and data warehousing principles.

Experience supporting database migration or reporting migration projects.

Strong communication, analytical, and documentation skills.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with multiple database platforms such as SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL, Snowflake, or BigQuery.

Familiarity with ETL/ELT processes and data governance practices.

Exposure to Python or R scripting.

Knowledge of version control and deployment best practices.

Experience with advanced analytics or statistical methods.

Title: Analyst, Data Governance
Description:
What You Will Do


Data Governance Leadership: Support the development, implementation, maintenance, monitoring, and enforcement of data governance standards, policies, procedures, and controls to ensure adherence to privacy, compliance, risk, regulatory, and legal requirements. This includes AI Governance to ensure ethical use and compliance with regulatory standards for AI-driven processes.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Collaborate with leadership, IT departments, business units, Legal, Compliance, Privacy, Risk teams, data producers and consumers, and IT data platform teams to align on data governance goals and support the operationalization of a robust data governance framework.
Promote data literacy by developing materials and conducting training on best practices.
Policy Development: Assist in the creation and enforcement of data management policies to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
Contribute to shaping the data governance accountabilities and operating model and enable data stewardship.
Data Quality Assurance: Contribute to the enhancement and implementation of robust data quality frameworks and conduct regular audits to maintain high data standards.
Support the development and evolution of data governance metrics and KPIs; monitor & measure the overall efficacy of the data governance program.
Proactively monitor data, metadata, and reference data to ensure quality across the CTB data lifecycle.
Implement and leverage data governance tools and platforms to enable capabilities; manage and maintain metadata and other data governance artifacts such as glossaries, classifications, and lineage.
Risk Management: Identify potential risks related to data governance and develop mitigation strategies.
Support data sharing and collaboration across teams and departments; ensure proper data access, usability, and monitoring of data sharing agreements.
Project Oversight: Contribute to large-scale data governance projects from inception to completion.
Stay updated on industry trends, standards, and regulatory requirements related to data governance; recommend appropriate modifications to existing policies and practices.


What You Bring


Post-secondary education in Computer Science, Information Systems Management, or a related field.
Proven experience with corporate data environments supporting cross-functional governance.
Proficient in PL\SQL and confident working in a structured data environment.
Knowledge of business intelligence, modern data warehousing techniques, cloud solutions such as Azure or GCP.
Experience with ETL/ELT processes and modern integration frameworks like API and event streaming.
Experience in IBM Cloud Pak for Data and/or Databricks Unity Catalog.
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, and Excel.
Expertise in creating/enforcing data governance policies to ensure data integrity, privacy, compliance, security.
Ability to work with Legal, Privacy, Compliance, and Risk partners
Hands-on experience with data analysis, quality assessment, metadata management.
Excellent communication skills for presenting complex concepts to non-technical audiences.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail; ability to problem-solve and handle ambiguity.
Effective stakeholder management; ability to build trusted relationships and influence decision-making.
Experience in process design/improvement initiatives and leveraging automation.
CDMP certification is an asset.


We’re always looking for great talent! In addition to competitive pay, we offer:


Comprehensive benefits and retirement programs
Performance incentives, Continuing Education Programs
Other perks to support your well-being
Career growth opportunities and product discounts


Our typical hiring range is between $53,000 and $88,000 Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, job-related knowledge, skills and competencies, market location, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements.

About Us

At Canadian Tire Services Limited/Canadian Tire Bank, it is our mandate to continue to create innovative and rewarding financial solutions for our customers. Our growing suite of products and services showcase the dynamic contributions from our employees and our success is driven by a strong vision, loyal customers, and our ability to build teams that reflect the diverse customers and communities in which we live and work. Join us, where there's a place for you here.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging

We are committed to fostering an environment where belonging thrives, and diversity, inclusion and equity are infused into everything we do. We believe in building an organizational culture where people are consistently treated with dignity while respecting individual religion, nationality, gender, race, age, perceived ability, spoken language, sexual orientation, and identification. We are united in our purpose of being here to help make life in Canada better. .

Accommodations

We stand firm in our Core Value that inclusion is a must. We welcome and encourage candidates from equity-seeking groups such as people who identify as racialized, Indigenous, 2SLGBTQIA+, women, people with disabilities, and beyond. Should you require any accommodation in applying for this role, or throughout the interview process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to help meet your needs.

HTML:
What You Will Do

  • Data Governance Leadership: Support the development, implementation, maintenance, monitoring, and enforcement of data governance standards, policies, procedures, and controls to ensure adherence to privacy, compliance, risk, regulatory, and legal requirements. This includes AI Governance to ensure ethical use and compliance with regulatory standards for AI-driven processes.
  • Stakeholder Collaboration: Collaborate with leadership, IT departments, business units, Legal, Compliance, Privacy, Risk teams, data producers and consumers, and IT data platform teams to align on data governance goals and support the operationalization of a robust data governance framework.
  • Promote data literacy by developing materials and conducting training on best practices.
  • Policy Development: Assist in the creation and enforcement of data management policies to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
  • Contribute to shaping the data governance accountabilities and operating model and enable data stewardship.
  • Data Quality Assurance: Contribute to the enhancement and implementation of robust data quality frameworks and conduct regular audits to maintain high data standards.
  • Support the development and evolution of data governance metrics and KPIs; monitor & measure the overall efficacy of the data governance program.
  • Proactively monitor data, metadata, and reference data to ensure quality across the CTB data lifecycle.
  • Implement and leverage data governance tools and platforms to enable capabilities; manage and maintain metadata and other data governance artifacts such as glossaries, classifications, and lineage.
  • Risk Management: Identify potential risks related to data governance and develop mitigation strategies.
  • Support data sharing and collaboration across teams and departments; ensure proper data access, usability, and monitoring of data sharing agreements.
  • Project Oversight: Contribute to large-scale data governance projects from inception to completion.
  • Stay updated on industry trends, standards, and regulatory requirements related to data governance; recommend appropriate modifications to existing policies and practices.

What You Bring

  • Post-secondary education in Computer Science, Information Systems Management, or a related field.
  • Proven experience with corporate data environments supporting cross-functional governance.
  • Proficient in PL\SQL and confident working in a structured data environment.
  • Knowledge of business intelligence, modern data warehousing techniques, cloud solutions such as Azure or GCP.
  • Experience with ETL/ELT processes and modern integration frameworks like API and event streaming.
  • Experience in IBM Cloud Pak for Data and/or Databricks Unity Catalog.
  • Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Visio, PowerPoint, and Excel.
  • Expertise in creating/enforcing data governance policies to ensure data integrity, privacy, compliance, security.
  • Ability to work with Legal, Privacy, Compliance, and Risk partners
  • Hands-on experience with data analysis, quality assessment, metadata management.
  • Excellent communication skills for presenting complex concepts to non-technical audiences.
  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail; ability to problem-solve and handle ambiguity.
  • Effective stakeholder management; ability to build trusted relationships and influence decision-making.
  • Experience in process design/improvement initiatives and leveraging automation.
  • CDMP certification is an asset.

We’re always looking for great talent! In addition to competitive pay, we offer:

  • Comprehensive benefits and retirement programs
  • Performance incentives, Continuing Education Programs
  • Other perks to support your well-being
  • Career growth opportunities and product discounts

Our typical hiring range is between $53,000 and $88,000 Salary decisions are also dependent on other factors such as your experience, job-related knowledge, skills and competencies, market location, industry benchmarks, internal equity and other role-specific requirements.

About Us

At Canadian Tire Services Limited/Canadian Tire Bank, it is our mandate to continue to create innovative and rewarding financial solutions for our customers. Our growing suite of products and services showcase the dynamic contributions from our employees and our success is driven by a strong vision, loyal customers, and our ability to build teams that reflect the diverse customers and communities in which we live and work. Join us, where there's a place for you here.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging

We are committed to fostering an environment where belonging thrives, and diversity, inclusion and equity are infused into everything we do. We believe in building an organizational culture where people are consistently treated with dignity while respecting individual religion, nationality, gender, race, age, perceived ability, spoken language, sexual orientation, and identification. We are united in our purpose of being here to help make life in Canada better. .

Accommodations

We stand firm in our Core Value that inclusion is a must. We welcome and encourage candidates from equity-seeking groups such as people who identify as racialized, Indigenous, 2SLGBTQIA+, women, people with disabilities, and beyond. Should you require any accommodation in applying for this role, or throughout the interview process, please make them known when contacted and we will work with you to help meet your needs.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Mekanism is a full-service modern agency making unexpected ideas unstoppable through the power of Soul & Science. We partner with brands to unleash breakthrough creativity and drive impact across the entire customer journey, delivering brand building at full volume.

With eight offices spanning markets throughout the US and Canada, we serve as collaborative business partners to some of the world’s best North American brands, including Alaska Airlines, Jose Cuervo, Quaker, Ben & Jerry's, and Amtrak in the US, and CAMH, Koodo Mobile and Public Mobile in Canada.

Named a Top 10 Most Effective Independent Agency in the US by the Effie Index, Mekanism has been profiled by The New York Times, Fast Company, and ABC’s Nightline. Our campaign methodologies are studied by Harvard Business School. For more information, visit Mekanism.com.

We are seeking a brilliant individual who loves data and analytics, is a master of spreadsheets, knows how to use SQL and understands tracking and measurement for media data architecture. This person will play an essential role in supporting the Data Infrastructure and Analytics team bridging the gap between complex big data environments and actionable media strategy, supporting our clients through advanced reporting, custom database solutions, and the implementation of cutting-edge AI agentic projects.

Responsibilities


Measurement Strategy: Partner with stakeholders to translate business goals into robust KPI frameworks and measurement plans for multi-channel media campaigns.
Data Engineering & Extraction: Extract, filter, and aggregate complex datasets using SQL. You will assess system capabilities and workflows to determine the feasibility of automated reporting solutions.
Visualization & Insights: Build and maintain high-impact client dashboards using internal and external tools like Looker Studio, Tableau, or Power BI. You'll turn raw data into visual stories with custom graphs.
Digital Architecture & Tracking: Ensure data integrity by managing pixel implementation, tag management (GTM, Adobe Launch), and URL parameter tracking (UTMs).
AI & Innovation: Support the development of AI agentic workflows and database solutions that drive efficiency for both internal teams and external clients.
Quality Assurance: Conduct rigorous data audits and quality control to ensure 100% accuracy in client deliverables.
Other duties may be assigned to meet the needs of the client, the team, and the company.


Qualifications


Minimum 1+ years of experience in data analytics. Experience within a media agency or digital marketing environment is strongly preferred.
Proven experience in SQL database design, coding, and maintenance. Mastery of Google Sheets/Excel (advanced formulas and pivot tables) is a must.
Cloud Fluency. Experience with Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery, Cloud Storage, Firebase) is highly desirable.
Proficiency with web analytics tools (Google Analytics 4, Adobe Analytics) and Tag Management systems.
Visualization Expertise. Strong portfolio in data visualization (Looker Studio, Tableau, or Power BI).
A proactive mindset with the ability to present complex technical ideas to non-technical stakeholders.
Preferred Assets:
Familiarity with AI implementation, including chatbots and agentic workflows.
Experience in front-end or back-end web development with working knowledge of JavaScript, HTML and CSS.
Working knowledge of Python and advanced statistical modeling skills
Understanding of GCP Vertex AI, Cloud Functions and Firebase.

Transparency matters to us. For this role, you can expect a salary between $50,000-$60,000 annually, depending on experience. This is a new vacancy

What’s in it for you:

We Value The ‘whole You’


An innovative and entrepreneurial work environment that promotes ingenuity and individuality - with the best of colleagues, and the best of clients
Hybrid work models and flexible hours, plus a work from anywhere policy for short-term stays in other regions
Generous Vacation starting at 3 weeks + 1 extra week over the holidays
Agency summer days (exact policy differs per campus and agency)
Modern, open co-working spaces with coffee & snack bars, located in great city neighborhoods, close to shops and restaurants and easy access to public transport
Dogs allowed, dedicated Play Room to support families and Private wellness room (in select offices)
Team social events - holiday parties, seasonal gatherings, weekly happy hours, inspiring speakers and more!


We are obsessed with learning and being better humans


In-house learning & development sessions and annual career and growth conversations’
Access to internal network of opportunities across all agencies
Focus on developing BIMPOC Talent via our BIMPOC Mentorship Program
Internal EDI group(s), programs & initiatives across Plus Company and opportunities to be involved in employee resource group(s)


Check out our EDI report here to learn more about our work and commitment towards equity.

We invest in your health and wellness:


Flexible benefits to support your individual lifestyle and life stage – with access to a personal wellness spending account you can use for a wide range of wellness expenses, including gym membership fees, pet care, education expenses, and much more!
Comprehensive mental health support including access to a virtual mental health therapy clinic providing you with personalized therapist guided iCBT programs and live therapy, access to health clinicians 24/7, and a holistic array of other health and wellness resources to support you and your family.
Dedicated play rooms for days you need to bring the kids and health and wellness rooms when you need a place to get grounded.
A responsible sick & personal absence days policy designed so you don't have to worry the moment a personal obligation or emergency arises.
Maternity leave top up program.


Expiry Date Notice: This job description expires on July 12, 2026

We’re humbled by the number of talented people who apply to work with us every day. And, we thank each and every one of you. Truly. We’ll reach out directly to those we’d like to meet for an interview.

To ensure a fair and consistent evaluation for all candidates, our interviewers are supported by an AI tool that securely takes notes during the conversation

Creating meaningful work means embracing different perspectives. We are an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate based on age, colour, ability, national origin, race, religion, sex, or sexual orientation. Not only are these provisions protected by provincial and federal law, embracing difference is what makes Mekanism what it is—and we wouldn’t have it any other way. We pride ourselves on our inclusive and accessible workplace. If you need any accommodations during the interview process to make it work for you, let us know.

By joining Mekanism, you become part of Plus Company, a unique partnership of forward-thinking creative agencies. As a network, we unabashedly believe anything is possible. Why? Because possibility is what powers the big dreams and limitless drive of an entrepreneur. And entrepreneurial agencies are what we’re made of. A whole network of them.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About GoBolt

Our Mission:

At GoBolt, we provide a technology-driven, customer-centric and sustainable approach to ecommerce and last-mile logistics for businesses of all sizes – from local brands to large national retailers. By operating as an extension of our customer's team, we deliver best-in-class services every step of the way. We're building North America’s largest electric vehicle fleet to help bring sustainability to an industry notorious for its heavy impact on the environment. We are in high growth mode and building for the future, which is where you come in!

We are on the lookout for a skilled, operationally minded, Data Analyst. This role will play a critical role in building and owning dashboards, metrics, and data models to support our operations and account management teams.

You will be responsible for deeply understanding our core metrics and ensuring we have actionable and insightful dashboards and data models to support our business. This includes dashboard development, data modelling, data cleanup, and ad-hoc analysis and reporting. This role will work very closely with our operations team and require some time spent in our warehouses.

Base Salary Range: 70k-80k

We are actively hiring to fill this specific position immediately.

Hybrid role based in Downtown Toronto

What You'll Do

Develop and Maintain Our Data Platform:


Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and maintain our analytics platform.
Identify and source new data to enrich our existing data platform.
Partner with our product and engineering teams to build data pipelines and integrations for seamless data ingestion.
Implement data governance practices to ensure data quality, consistency, and security.


Measure And Report On Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)


Maintain our source of truth documentation for KPI definitions and data sources across the business.
Ensure core dashboards are well-maintained and groomed to reduce conflicting information, streamline data usage, and drive actionable insights.
Conduct ad-hoc analysis to support operational efficiency and revenue retention.


Troubleshoot And Clean Up Data


Monitor the data platform for bugs, anomalies, or data quality issues.
Investigate and troubleshoot issues related to data accuracy, completeness, or consistency.
Conduct data cleaning and normalization to ensure data integrity and reliability.


What We're Looking For


1-2 years of experience as a data analyst or in a similar role.
Strong proficiency in SQL for querying, transforming, and manipulating data
Experience with a Business Intelligence tool: You are comfortable building dashboards and reports in BI tools(Looker, Tableau, Mode, Metabase etc.). We use Looker and Metabase at GoBolt
A background in Computer Science, Analytics, or a related field.
Excellent communication skills to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and explain complex concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
A willingness to go deep into our operations to understand processes and metrics and help shape their development over time.


Bonus Points For


Experience with operations and logistics, having handled data related to inventory, warehousing, last-mile delivery, and transportation.
Familiarity with data modeling concepts and best practices, especially within a supply chain or logistics context.
Experience with dbt (data build tool) for modular and version-controlled data transformations
Proficiency in Python and scripting, including integrating data from external sources.
Familiarity with version control, with a working knowledge of Git for collaborative development.
Experience with cloud computing platforms like GCP or AWS


Why Join Us?

We are innovative, goal-oriented, and are always on the search for energetic and passionate people who are eager to learn and would like to have a rare opportunity to grow within one of Canada's fastest-growing companies.

We offer a workplace where respect, diversity, and inclusion are the cornerstones of our culture. Our team members are encouraged to bring their authentic selves to work, contributing to our collective mission in their unique ways. Aside from an amazing culture, we also offer competitive salaries and a comprehensive benefits package including health, dental, and vision plans for you and your family.

If you are eager to make a difference and contribute to a fast paced exciting environment, we would love to hear from you.

GoBolt is an equal-opportunity employer that recognizes the value and importance of a diverse workforce. GoBolt is committed to providing assistance during all phases of the employment cycle, including recruiting through to the end of the employment relationship. If you require accommodation because of injury, illness, or disability during any portion of GoBolt’s recruiting process, please let us know by contacting your recruiter's point of contact, and we will do everything we can to accommodate you.

At GoBolt, we value innovation and efficiency. In line with Ontario’s Working for Workers Act, we utilize AI-powered tools to assist in the screening and initial assessment of candidates for this position. This helps us focus on your skills and reduces bias. While AI helps us get started, your journey with us is guided by real people. Every final decision is made by a member of our team who values the unique perspective you bring.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Hi

I hope you are doing well.

We have an urgent position listed below. Please send your most recent resume along with the expected rate if you are interested.

Job Role: Data Analyst

Location: Mississauga, ON, Canada (Hybrid)

Job Description:

Experience Level – 8 to 10 years

Data Analyst Job Description

Role Summary

The Data Analyst will be responsible for translating raw data into actionable insights, providing data-driven recommendations to support business strategy and decision-making. This role involves collecting, cleaning, analysing, and visualising data to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for process and performance improvement.




Collect clean and analyze operational data and generate dashboards and reports tracking KPIs (e.g. order ingestion volumes, error rates , SLA adherence)
Conduct trend analysis and performance monitoring
Provide recommendations for process improvements based on data insights
Collaborate with Operational Analyst and Operation Managers for data validation




Key Responsibilities and Duties

Data Acquisition and Preparation:
Extract, Transform, and Load (ETL) data from various primary and secondary data sources (e.g., internal databases, cloud platforms, and external APIs).
Clean and preprocess raw data to ensure accuracy, consistency, and completeness, including identifying and correcting errors, managing missing data, and transforming formats.
Design and maintain databases (e.g., data warehouse models) and data systems to optimize data access and quality.
Data Analysis and Interpretation:
Apply statistical methods and data analysis techniques to perform exploratory and diagnostic analysis to uncover trends, anomalies, and relationships within complex datasets.
Conduct hypothesis testing and develop models (e.g., regression analysis) to support business questions and predict outcomes.
Define, track, and report on Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and metrics across various business functions (e.g., Marketing, Sales, Operations).
Reporting and Visualisation:
Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using visualization tools to present data in a clear, digestible format for both technical and non-technical audiences



Collaboration and Improvement:
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g., Data Engineering, Product, IT) to understand data requirements and deliver targeted, timely insights.
Proactively identify and recommend process improvements, system modifications, and operational changes based on data analysis.




Required Qualifications and Skills

Essential Technical Skills (Hard Skills)

SQL (Structured Query Language): Advanced proficiency in writing complex queries, stored procedures, and managing relational databases.
Statistical Programming: Expertise in at least one statistical programming language (Python or R) for complex data analysis, statistical modelling, and automation (e.g., using libraries like Pandas, NumPy, and Scikit-learn).
Data Visualisation Tools: High proficiency in industry-standard tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Google Looker Studio for creating interactive dashboards.
Spreadsheet Tools: Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUPs, functions, and modelling).
Statistical Analysis: Strong foundation in descriptive and inferential statistics.

Essential Workplace Skills (Soft Skills)

Critical Thinking and Problem-Solving: The ability to approach a business problem logically, ask the right questions, and find meaningful patterns in data.
Communication and Presentation: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to clearly articulate complex technical information and its business implications.
Attention to Detail: Meticulous approach to data cleaning and quality assurance to ensure the highest level of data integrity and reporting accuracy.

Business Acumen: Understanding of business processes and the industry to ensure data analysis is relevant and actionable.

  • Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using visualization tools to present data in a clear, digestible format for both technical and non-technical audiences



  • Collaboration and Improvement:
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams (e.g., Data Engineering, Product, IT) to understand data requirements and deliver targeted, timely insights.
  • Proactively identify and recommend process improvements, system modifications, and operational changes based on data analysis.


Required Qualifications and Skills

Essential Technical Skills (Hard Skills)

  • SQL (Structured Query Language): Advanced proficiency in writing complex queries, stored procedures, and managing relational databases.
  • Statistical Programming: Expertise in at least one statistical programming language (Python or R) for complex data analysis, statistical modelling, and automation (e.g., using libraries like Pandas, NumPy, and Scikit-learn).
  • Data Visualisation Tools: High proficiency in industry-standard tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Google Looker Studio for creating interactive dashboards.
  • Spreadsheet Tools: Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUPs, functions, and modelling).
  • Statistical Analysis: Strong foundation in descriptive and inferential statistics.

Essential Workplace Skills (Soft Skills)

  • Critical Thinking and Problem-Solving: The ability to approach a business problem logically, ask the right questions, and find meaningful patterns in data.
  • Communication and Presentation: Excellent verbal and written communication skills to clearly articulate complex technical information and its business implications.
  • Attention to Detail: Meticulous approach to data cleaning and quality assurance to ensure the highest level of data integrity and reporting accuracy.

Business Acumen: Understanding of business processes and the industry to ensure data analysis is relevant and actionable.

Title: Compliance Data & Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Location: Toronto, ON | Hybrid (Open to candidates across Ontario)

Language: English, strong written and verbal communication required. French is considered an asset.

Background Check Requirement: Successful completion of a background check may be required.

About the Opportunity

Our client is seeking a Compliance Data & Business Intelligence Analyst to join a collaborative team focused on supporting environmental stewardship and regulatory compliance across Ontario. This role offers the opportunity to combine data analytics, business intelligence, compliance reporting, and policy interpretation to support evidence-based decision-making within a highly regulated environment.




Reporting to the Manager, Compliance Data and Business Intelligence, you will work closely with compliance, analytics, finance, IT, and external stakeholders to ensure data integrity, improve reporting processes, and deliver actionable insights that support program performance, regulatory oversight, and continuous improvement initiatives.




What's in it for You

Opportunity to contribute to environmental sustainability and circular economy initiatives
Exposure to complex regulatory programs and high-impact data-driven projects
Collaborative and mission-driven team environment
Opportunity to influence decision-making through analytics, reporting, and business intelligence
A workplace that values continuous learning, innovation, and professional development




Your Responsibilities

You'll develop and maintain compliance reporting processes, dashboards, and performance metrics across regulated programs.
You'll analyze compliance, operational, and program data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement.
You'll act as a data steward, ensuring data quality, governance, documentation, and reporting consistency.
You'll support KPI development, performance measurement, and executive reporting to monitor program effectiveness.
You'll collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to understand reporting requirements and deliver meaningful business insights.
You'll support cross-functional initiatives, including system enhancements, process improvements, budgeting activities, and public reporting.
You'll conduct data reviews and compliance assessments to support regulatory oversight and operational decision-making.




Skills and Qualifications

4+ years of progressive experience in business intelligence, data analytics, reporting, compliance analytics, or program analysis.
Experience working in a regulated, compliance-driven, public sector, environmental, or governance-focused environment.
Advanced proficiency with Excel, CRM systems (Salesforce preferred), reporting tools, and data visualization platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex data into actionable recommendations.
Experience supporting data governance, data quality management, and reporting best practices.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills with the ability to present findings to technical and non-technical audiences.
Knowledge of waste management, environmental policy, regulatory frameworks, or compliance programs is considered a strong asset.




Note from the Hiring Manager

"We're looking for someone who enjoys digging into data, identifying trends, and transforming information into meaningful insights. Success in this role comes from being curious, detail-oriented, and passionate about using data to support informed decision-making and program improvement."




Why Partner with Altis

If you’ve never worked with a staffing agency before, we make it easy. We work with top employers across Canada who have great jobs to fill, each vetted and verified by our team. When you apply for a job with Altis, we get to know you as a candidate and learn what your strengths are. Then, if you’re a solid match, we handle all the logistics, advocating for you as a candidate for the role, providing access to coaching and connecting you directly with the hiring manager. And rest assured, all our services are free of cost for candidates.

Title: Analyst, Data Insights
Description:
Job Description

What is the opportunity?

In the increasingly complex world of financial services, accurate and complete data is critical for risk management and regulatory compliance. Ensuring data integrity begins with an understanding of financial services data, data quality controls and identifying and resolving data observations and issues. As an Analyst, Data Insights, you will be accountable for driving improvements in Risk and Finance data through the management of identification, root cause analysis, and resolution of data issues for US business critical processes and regulatory reports. Reporting to the Associate Director, you will be responsible in building trust and ensuring the accuracy and completeness of shared data assets and reporting data by managing data quality initiatives.

What will you do?

Data Quality monitoring, observation identification and management


Review assessment of exceptions and observations management investigations
Reporting, overseeing and monitoring the remediation of data and processes, as required.


Investigations and reporting


Manages and conducts detailed investigations and report findings based on data exceptions, identified observations, and ad hoc requests from critical Report owners and other users of shared data assets.
Provide monthly summary and detailed data quality reporting to business partners, stakeholders and executive leadership, including data quality exceptions, findings and the status of outstanding observations.


Continuous improvement


Regularly review and update data quality process documentation for accuracy and completeness, monitor their efficiency and effectiveness and recommend/implement improvements to controls, investigations, reporting, observations management and work flows.
Based on root-cause analysis findings, propose process changes/improvements to address gaps or inconsistencies in data, processes and/or controls and raise awareness of best practices and approved provisioning points for commonly used data assets.
Maintain data quality tests and data quality observation repositories
Ongoing evaluation of rules to ensure high quality rules are reported to stakeholders


Leadership and collaboration


Communicate effectively with team members, stakeholders and business partners, resolving conflicts/issues of medium complexity and escalating to others as appropriate.
Bridge the gap between technical requirements and business teams to facilitate common understanding


What do you need to succeed?

Must-have


2 + years of experience in financial services and systems including finance and credit risk data, data quality principles, data management, business analysis.
Knowledge of financial reporting and credit risk business concepts and procedures.
Intermediate to advanced knowledge of data analysis methods and the ability to analyze and resolve complex data related issues.
Experience conducting root cause analysis, using tools such as SQL, Excel.
Experience with business and/or technical documentation and the assessment of existing processes and systems.


Nice-to-have


Degree in mathematics, statistics, engineering, or related quantitative field
Ability to manage multiple competing priorities in times of stress.
Experience working with financial services and systems including finance and/or credit risk data
Experience using Collibra or a similar data management or workflow platform


What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.


A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits and competitive compensation
Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
Flexible work/life balance options
Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
Opportunities to do challenging work


Job Skills

Critical Thinking, Data Administration, Database Structures, Data Modeling, Data Movement, Detail-Oriented, Group Problem Solving, Information Capture, Standard Operating Procedure (SOP), Teamwork

Title: AI Productivity Analyst, Data Analytics
Description:
AI Productivity Analyst, Data Analytics

Location: Ontario (Toronto preferred)

Contract: 6 months




Pay Rates:

T4: $57.71 (T4 only)




What You'll Do

Partner with Product Managers, Engineering leaders, and cross-functional teams to define business questions, KPIs, and analytical frameworks for AI and productivity initiatives.
Analyze large, complex datasets to understand AI adoption, user behavior, workflow efficiency, and business outcomes.
Identify trends, opportunities, and friction points that influence how teams and customers interact with AI-enabled capabilities.
Develop dashboards and visualizations using tools such as Power BI, Looker, or similar BI platforms to communicate insights effectively.
Apply statistical analysis, forecasting, experimentation, and predictive modeling techniques to evaluate initiative performance and guide investment decisions.
Build trusted datasets, semantic layers, and scalable analytics models that enable self-service reporting and informed decision-making.
Ensure data quality and integrity by implementing best practices in data collection, transformation, and governance.
Translate technical findings into clear, actionable recommendations for both technical and non-technical stakeholders.




What You'll Bring

8+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, or related analytical roles, ideally within a technology or SaaS environment.
Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or another quantitative discipline.
Hands-on experience using AI tools such as Claude Code, Cursor, or similar to enhance productivity, automate analysis, and prototype solutions.
Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python or R for data analysis, statistical modeling, and automation.
Experience working with modern data stacks and ELT technologies such as Snowflake, dbt, Airflow, or similar platforms.
Proven experience designing scalable analytics data models and governed datasets that support enterprise reporting and decision-making.
Expertise with visualization tools such as Looker, Power BI, or equivalent BI platforms.
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure.
Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving abilities, and the ability to independently drive insights from ambiguous or complex datasets.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to influence stakeholders across technical and business teams.




Nice to Have

Experience working within SaaS companies or Platform Engineering organizations.
Familiarity with Autodesk products and the industries they serve.
Master's degree in a quantitative discipline.

Title: Data Analyst (Financial Planning and Analysis) - Canada
Description:
Tiger Analytics is an advanced analytics consulting firm. We are the trusted analytics partner for several Fortune 100 companies, enabling them to generate business value from data. Our consultants bring deep expertise in Data Science, Data Analytics, Data Engineering, Business Intelligence, Machine Learning and AI. Various market research firms, including Forrester and Gartner have recognized our business value and leadership.

We are seeking a highly motivated Data Analyst with strong data engineering and analytics experience to support business stakeholders in driving data-driven decision-making. The ideal candidate will possess strong expertise in SQL, Snowflake, Databricks, and Python, along with hands-on experience working with financial reconciliation data, including transaction, ledger, and procurement datasets

Job Duties:


Partner with business stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document data requirements
Perform data profiling, data mapping, and data quality assessments across multiple source systems
Develop complex SQL queries and data models to support reporting, reconciliation, and analytical initiatives
Work with Snowflake and Databricks to design, transform, and manage large-scale datasets
Build and maintain Python-based data processing and validation workflows
Analyze financial reconciliation data, including transactions, general ledger, accounts payable, procurement, and related financial datasets
Identify data discrepancies and support reconciliation processes through root cause analysis
Collaborate with business, data engineering, and technology teams to ensure data accuracy and consistency
Support data governance, metadata management, and documentation activities


Requirements


6+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Business Intelligence, or Data Engineering roles
Strong hands-on experience with SQL, Snowflake, Databricks, and Python
Experience in data mapping, data profiling, data validation, and data quality management
Mandatory experience working with financial reconciliation data
Strong understanding of transaction-level data, general ledger (GL), procurement, accounts payable/receivable, and financial reporting processes
Experience supporting finance, accounting, or enterprise reconciliation initiatives is highly preferred
Experience working in cloud-based data environments
Exposure to data governance and master data management practices
Familiarity with financial controls, audit, or reconciliation frameworks


Benefits

Significant career development opportunities exist as the company grows. The position offers a unique opportunity to be part of a small, fast-growing, challenging and entrepreneurial environment, with a high degree of individual responsibility.

Tiger Analytics provides equal employment opportunities to applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, marital status, protected veteran status, disability status, or any other basis as protected by federal, state, or local law.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Requisition ID: 261927

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Data Engineer plays a critical role within the Enterprise Data & AI Technology organization—one of Scotiabank’s most significant enterprise-wide strategic initiatives. This organization drives data enabled decision making, AI innovation, and technology modernization across the Bank.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:


You will design, build, and maintain scalable, resilient data pipelines to support the ingestion, processing, and distribution of large datasets.
You will leverage SaaS platforms and tools to develop, configure, and automate data workflows, improving efficiency across the data engineering lifecycle.
You will partner with stakeholders and product managers to gather requirements, design ingestion patterns, and onboard new data sources into the cloud data platform.
You will implement observability capabilities and continuously optimize application services and pipeline performance.
You will perform data integrity validation to ensure accuracy, reliability, and consistency of data.


Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We'd love to work with you if you have:


Experience building data pipelines, and composable cloud-based data platforms (Azure), using Azure Data Factory, ADLS.
Experience working with Lakehouse platforms: Databricks, Unity Catalog, Auto Loader
Experience with data engineering, programming, ETL and ELT processes for data extraction and processing
Experience working with structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data.
Strong programming and scripting skills (SQL, Python, Java, Scala)
Strong knowledge of cloud infrastructure and microservices architecture

Title: Data Analyst, Credit Risk Management Department
Company: Industrial and Commercial Bank of China (Canada)
Description:
Department / Branch: Credit Risk Management Department

Reports To: Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department

Employment Type: Full-Time Contract/ Permanent

Location: Richmond Hill Office







Responsibilities

1. Model Development and Optimization

Design, develop, and optimize credit rating models to solve business problems.
Experiment with algorithms, architectures, and frameworks to improve model performance.
Conduct regular validation and tuning of models for accuracy and efficiency.

2. Data Analysis

Work with large datasets, ensuring quality, consistency, and relevance for training models.
Identify patterns and insights to refine model inputs and features.

3. Model Evaluation and Testing

Implement evaluation metrics and validation techniques to ensure model reliability.
Perform testing to compare different models and configurations.

4. Monitoring and Reporting

Deploy models to production environments and monitor their real-time performance.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues to maintain model robustness over time.
Conduct model performance monitoring and reporting.

5. Collaboration and Communication

Collaborate with data stakeholders to align model objectives with business goals.
Communicate findings, methodologies, and results to technical and non-technical audiences .or regulator.

6. Other works assigned by Head and Senior Manager of Credit Risk Management Department.




Qualifications:

Bachelor degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Data Science, Applied Mathematics, Machine Learning, Financial Engineering or related fields.
At least 3 years of experience in financial modelling, preferably within banking or financial services sector.
Strong knowledge in model development, validation, evaluation and optimization.
Proficient in Python, R or similar programming languages.
Excellent problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
Strong communication and teamwork abilities.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English with work proficiency.
Fluency in Chinese is an asset.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Who We Are

Konrad is a next-generation digital consultancy. We are dedicated to solving complex business problems for our global clients with creative and forward-thinking solutions. Our employees enjoy a culture built on innovation and a commitment to creating best-in-class digital products in use by hundreds of millions of consumers around the world. We hire exceptionally smart, analytical, and hard-working people who are lifelong learners.

About The Role

This role sits on our Digital team, where your internal title will be Specialist, Digital, working primarily in a data analytics capacity across client engagements.

As a Specialist, Digital focused on Data Analytics, you are an early-to-mid career data professional with a strong foundation in analysis and a desire to grow both your technical and consulting skill set. Leveraging your analytics background, you will support the delivery of digital products for our clients through analytics and reporting solutions that help organizations better understand their data and make informed decisions.

In addition to your analytics work, you will gain exposure to broader areas of digital consulting, including digital product development, digital transformation, and user experience (UX/CX). You are interested in understanding how data supports product decisions, customer experiences, and overall business strategy.

You will work closely with senior analysts, data scientists, engineers, product managers, designers, and business stakeholders across a variety of client engagements. With guidance and mentorship, you will contribute to analytics initiatives including dashboarding, exploratory analysis, KPI tracking, and data quality validation, while also building broader consulting skills across areas such as project management, user experience, and design thinking.

You are curious, detail-oriented, and motivated to continuously improve your analytical and consulting capabilities.

What You’ll Do


Support client and internal teams in understanding business questions and translating them into analytical requirements
Analyze data to identify trends, patterns, and insights under the guidance of senior team members Build and maintain dashboards and recurring reports to track key metrics and performance indicators
Assist in documenting metrics, definitions, and analytics processes
Collaborate with data engineers and other analysts to support data modeling and reporting layers Communicate insights clearly through visualizations, summaries, and presentations
Proactively identify opportunities to improve reporting, data quality, and analytical workflows


Qualifications


Minimum 2+ years of experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or analytics-focused role
Programming experience in Python, Bash scripting, R or related language
Working proficiency in SQL and experience querying cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Redshift, BigQuery)
Experience with Business Intelligence and data visualization tools (e.g., Looker, Tableau, Power BI)
Foundational understanding of data modeling and analytics best practices
Experience defining and tracking KPIs and metrics
A/B testing and causal analysis
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
Clear written and verbal communication skills Ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional and consulting-style environments
Interest in learning about data governance, privacy, and security best practices


Perks And Benefits


Comprehensive Health & Wellness Benefits Package
Retirement Planning
Parental Leave Program
Flexible Working Hours
Work from Home Flexibility
Service Recognition Programs
Socials, Outings & Retreats
Culture of Learning & Development


Bonus Points

Have you taken any courses at BrainStation? A lot of our design and development best practices and processes are taught during our courses, make sure to highlight this experience in your cover letter if you have!

Konrad is committed to maintaining a diverse work environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants, regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status will receive consideration for employment. If you have any accessibility requirements or concerns regarding the hiring process or employment with us, please notify us so we can provide suitable accommodation.

The estimated compensation for this position is $75,000 to $100,000. This is an estimate and a compensation offer will vary based on applicant’s education, experience, skills, abilities and alignment with market data.

While we sincerely appreciate all applications, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted.

Title: Data Reporting Analyst
Description:
Inclusion without Exception




Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is an equal opportunity employer, and embraces diversity in race, nationality, ethnicity, gender, age, physical ability, neurodiversity, and sexual orientation, to create a workforce that reflects the societies we operate in. Our continued commitment to Culture and Diversity is reflected in our people stories across our workforce and implemented through equitable workplace policies and processes.




Tata Consultancy Services (BSE: 532540, NSE: TCS) is the technology partner of choice for industry-leading organizations worldwide. Since its inception in 1968, TCS has upheld the highest standards of innovation, engineering excellence and customer service.




It has set an aspiration to become the world's largest AI-led technology services company and is enabling its clients to transform themselves across the full AI stack, from infrastructure to intelligence.




Rooted in the heritage of the Tata Group, TCS is focused on creating long term value for its clients, its investors, its employees, and the community at large. With a highly skilled workforce spread across 56 countries and 194 service delivery centers across the world, the company has been recognized as a top employer in six continents. With the ability to rapidly apply and scale new technologies, the company has built long term partnerships with its clients. Many of these relationships have endured into decades and navigated every technology cycle, from mainframes in the 1970s to artificial intelligence today.




Data & Reporting Analyst




Key Requirements

Develop complex SQL queries to extract, transform, and analyze data from multiple data sources
Design and build reporting solutions using tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar visualization platforms
Build and manage semantic layers to provide consistent and intuitive data models for reporting and self-service analytics
Optimize query performance by converting repetitive queries into reusable functions, stored procedures, and views
Execute database migration activities, including migrating stored procedures and reporting logic across platforms
Collaborate with business stakeholders, data engineers, and leadership to gather requirements and deliver analytical solutions
Ensure data quality, accuracy, consistency, and reliability across reporting outputs
Strong proficiency in SQL with experience in writing and optimizing complex queries
Hands-on experience with at least one major reporting or visualization tool (Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Knowledge of semantic layer design and data modeling
Experience with database migration projects and stored procedure conversion
Ability to refactor queries into structured and reusable components
Strong understanding of relational database concepts




Salary Range - CA$ 90,000 - CA$ 110,000 Per Year




TCS does not use artificial intelligence tools for candidate screening or evaluation. This post is for a current vacancy. The hiring process includes an initial screening, followed by a technical evaluation and managerial discussion.




Tata Consultancy Services Canada Inc. is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of all individuals in accordance with the Accessibility for Ontarians with Disabilities Act (AODA) and the Ontario Human Rights Code (OHRC). Should you require accommodation during the recruitment and selection process, please inform Human Resources.




Thank you for your interest in TCS. Candidates that meet the qualifications for this position will be contacted within a 2-week period. We invite you to continue to apply for other opportunities that match your profile.

Title: Commercial Business Analyst
Description:
Job Description



As a Commercial Business Analyst, your main focus will be to conduct customer segmentation, cohort analysis, and product performance reviews; Act as expert user of SQL and data platforms, supporting data extraction; and provide insights on campaign effectiveness, including conversion attribution and return on ad.



This on-site role reports into our Commercial Manager and is based out of the Toronto Support Office.



A Day in the Life



Working at H&M means no two days are ever the same, but a typical day will include the following responsibilities*



Insights and analytics
Conduct 360 analysis from customer, web and sales perspective to gather insights relevant for Commercial team
Analyze full funnel web performance across .com and app, including traffic, user behavior, product performance, and sales
Provide insights on campaign effectiveness, including conversion attribution and return on ad spend
Analyze sales, customer and web data to draw insights related to QCP, product, assortment and pricing
Plan, support, and evaluate A/B and multivariate testing to optimize customer experience and conversion in online store and CRM/Email
Conduct customer segmentation, cohort analysis, and product performance reviews
Implement tagging, tracking, and SEO analytics to ensure accurate data collection and improve visibility in owned channels
Act as expert user of SQL and data platforms, supporting data extraction and team upskilling
Collaboration
Generate regular reports and dashboards with actionable insights for stakeholders
Support Experience Lead, E-com Leads, Commercial Managers and Marketing team with analytics activities and share insights







Qualifications



Who You Are:



Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Data Science, Statistics, Economics, or related field
Proficiency in Google Analytics, Adobe Analytics, SQL, Tableau, and Power BI
Strong knowledge of A/B testing methodologies and web tracking implementation
Strong analytical, reporting, and presentation skills
2+ years’ web analytics experience, preferably in eCommerce or fashion retail







Additional Information



Why You’ll Love Working at H&M



We value diverse backgrounds, including but not limited to race, ethnicity, gender, age, religion, sexual orientation, and disability
We pride ourselves on being a values-driven organization, guided by our 7 unique values that have been part of our success story for over 75 years
You’ll have access to our Colleague Resource Groups (CRGs) to find community and networking opportunities among colleagues
You’ll be able to express your personal style with our employee discount at H&M, & Other Stories, and COS





Compensation: salary is $97,200



*This job posting highlights the most critical responsibilities and requirements of the job and is not all-inclusive. There may be additional duties, and responsibilities assigned for this job at the company’s discretion.

Title: Data Analyst & Data Modeler – Product & Insurance Risk
Description:
Our client is a top financial institution with significant North American holdings. They have operations across most major verticals, including institutional & corporate, wealth management, private clients, commercial banking, treasury, and retail banking.
 
Introduction:
Robertson is seeking a skilled Data Analyst & Data Modeler – Product & Insurance Risk to join our client in support of an existing vacancy.

Contract Period: 7 months with potential to extend
Pay Rate: Starting from $50 per hour
Location: Toronto, ON
Location Type: Hybrid
Business Hours: Monday-Friday; core business hours
 
Job Responsibilities:
Analyse and document business processes for insurance product development, underwriting assessments, risk scoring, reinsurance allocations, and claims
Collaborate with actuarial, underwriting, risk, and claims teams to capture
Translate complex insurance risk rules into detailed Business Requirements
Analyze and model data related to insurance products, underwriting risk scores,
reinsurance structures, and claims lifecycle events
Develop conceptual, logical, and physical data models supporting insurance risk analytics and reporting layers
Support development and enhancement of Product & Insurance Risk frameworks that align with regulatory expectations and internal governance
Assist in mapping reinsurance treaties, ceded and assumed reinsurance rules, and integration touchpoints across policy admin, underwriting, and claims systems
Document reinsurance business processes, bordereaux reporting requirements, and quality assurance checkpoints.
Data Mapping, Analytics & Reporting

Title: Data Analyst & Data Modeler – Bank/Finance - Power BI
Description:
Prior experience within the Banking or Financial Services sector is mandatory.




Position Overview :

We are seeking a highly skilled Data Analyst & Data Modeler with deep expertise in Product & Insurance Risk, specifically across Life & Health Insurance, Underwriting, Reinsurance, and Claims domains.

This role focuses on designing enterprise-grade data models, analytics pipelines, and reporting solutions to support Insurance claims analytics, and regulatory reporting.

The ideal candidate will combine strong data modeling and SQL expertise with advanced Power BI development skills, including Power BI Premium/Fabric architecture, Lakehouse, semantic modeling, and enterprise reporting design.




Key Responsibilities :

Insurance Risk Data Analysis & Modeling :

Analyze and document business processes for insurance product development, underwriting assessments, risk scoring, reinsurance allocations, and claims adjudication workflows.
Collaborate with actuarial, underwriting, risk, and claims teams to capture functional and non functional requirements.
Translate complex insurance risk rules into detailed Business Requirements Documents (BRDs), Functional Specifications (FSDs), and User Stories.
Analyze and model data related to insurance products, underwriting risk scores, reinsurance structures, and claims lifecycle events.
Develop conceptual, logical, and physical data models supporting insurance risk analytics and reporting layers.




Risk Governance & Reinsurance Integration :

Support development and enhancement of Product & Insurance Risk frameworks that align with regulatory expectations and internal governance.
Assist in mapping reinsurance treaties, ceded and assumed reinsurance rules, and integration touchpoints across policy admin, underwriting, and claims systems.
Document reinsurance business processes, bordereaux reporting requirements, and quality assurance checkpoints.




Data Mapping, Analytics & Reporting :

Develop source-to-target mappings for underwriting, reinsurance, and claims datasets across multiple systems (PAS, CRM, Rating Engines, Claims Platforms).
Support data quality checks, lineage documentation, and risk metric definitions (e.g., underwriting leakage, claims ratios, reinsurance, over retention).
Perform SQL-based data analysis to support underwriting rules, reserving calculations, risk exposure reporting, and claims trend analysis.




Systems, Platforms & Agile Delivery :

Work with cross-functional Agile squads to design workflows, integrations, and platform enhancements supporting underwriting, reinsurance, or claims modernization.
Participate in sprint planning, backlog refinement, and product grooming sessions.
Collaborate with architects and developers to validate technical feasibility of requirements and ensure alignment with architectural patterns.




Stakeholder & Vendor Engagement :

Serve as a primary liaison between business units (Underwriting, Actuarial, Claims, Reinsurance Operations) and IT delivery teams.
Coordinate with third-party vendors (reinsurance platforms, claims systems, fraud analytics tools, etc.) to support integration and system configuration activities.
Facilitate workshops, requirement walkthroughs, and UAT cycles to ensure clarity, completeness, and alignment.




Advanced Power BI & Fabric Development :

Design and develop enterprise-scale Power BI solutions, including:
Semantic models (star schema, dimensional models)
Complex DAX measures and calculation groups
Multi-layered datasets optimized for performance
Build and manage Power BI Premium capacity and workspace architecture:
Capacity planning and optimization
Deployment pipelines (Dev/Test/Prod)
Row-level security (RLS) and object-level security (OLS)




Microsoft Fabric & Modern Data Modeling :

Design and implement Fabric-based analytics solutions:
Lakehouse / Warehouse modeling
Delta tables and medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold layers)
Semantic model integration with Power BI
Optimize datasets for large-scale, near real-time analytics across insurance risk domains.




Experience Summary :

Hands-on experience in both Business Analysis (BA) activities and Power BI development.
Strong knowledge of Power BI data modeling, including working with Microsoft Fabric.
Experienced in coordinating with infrastructure teams on firewall configurations and enabling secure data connectivity.
Collaborated closely with data engineering teams to ensure efficient data pipelines, connectivity, and performance optimization for Power BI solutions.
Skilled in optimizing Power BI reports and dashboards to improve user experience and performance for end users.




Must-Have Skills :

Proven experience as a Power BI Developer, with up-to-date knowledge of the latest Power BI features and enhancements.
Strong proficiency in SQL and DAX for data transformation, querying, and advanced calculations.
Solid expertise in data modeling, including designing efficient and scalable semantic models.
Skilled in data analysis, with the ability to derive actionable insights from complex datasets.
Experience implementing and managing controls within Power BI, including governance, security, and performance optimization.




Nice-to-Have Skills :

Domain experience in the insurance industry is an asset.
Familiarity with insurance core systems such as:
Guidewire (PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, Reinsurance Management)
Life/Health Policy Administration Systems (e.g., ALIP, Ingenium, OIPA)
Underwriting workbenches or rating engines
Experience with data visualization tools such as Power BI and Tableau.




Years of Experience :

5+ years of experience




Education & Certifications :

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, Insurance, Risk Management, or related field.
Nice to have:
Professional certifications in Agile, Business Analysis (CBAP/PMI-PBA), Insurance (CIP/LOMA), or Risk Management (CRM/FRM).
Power BI certification (Microsoft Certifications).




Candidate Requirements :

Must have strong, hands-on expertise in Power BI, including thorough knowledge of the latest features and updates.
Candidates with only basic or limited Power BI experience will not be considered.
Requires deep expertise in advanced data modeling and building efficient, scalable semantic models.
Must be a Power BI and data modeling specialist, capable of designing optimized, high-performance reporting solutions.




The expected base salary range for this position is $50 – $60 per hour, depending on experience, skills, and internal equity.

Title: Data Analyst – Python, Spark & Databricks
Description:
Practice - AIA - Artificial Intelligence and Analytics

About AI & Analytics: Artificial intelligence (AI) and the data it collects and analyzes will soon sit at the core of all intelligent, human-centric businesses. By decoding customer needs, preferences, and behaviors, our clients can understand exactly what services, products, and experiences their consumers need. Within AI & Analytics, we work to design the future—a future in which trial-and-error business decisions have been replaced by informed choices and data-supported strategies.

By applying AI and data science, we help leading companies to prototype, refine, validate, and scale their AI and analytics products and delivery models. Cognizant’s AIA practice takes insights that are buried in data and provides businesses a clear way to transform how they source, interpret and consume their information. Our clients need flexible data structures and a streamlined data architecture that quickly turns data resources into informative, meaningful intelligence.


Please note, this role is not able to offer visa transfer or sponsorship now or in the future*


Job Summary

We are seeking a Data Analyst with strong Python and Spark skills to support data engineering and analytics initiatives. This role involves working with large datasets using Databricks, developing data processing logic, and collaborating with business and product teams to deliver data-driven solutions. You will play a key role in transforming business requirements into technical implementations while ensuring data accuracy and quality. The position requires hands-on coding, SQL expertise, and the ability to work in an Agile environment.

In This Role, You Will


Develop and maintain data processing workflows using Python and Spark on Databricks.
Collaborate with BSA and product teams to understand and translate requirements into technical solutions.
Write Python and SQL code to process, validate, and analyze data.
Maintain and update source-to-target mapping documents.
Perform unit testing and support defect resolution in collaboration with QA and business testers.
Coordinate with administrators for deployments and version control activities.
Work closely with offshore teams to ensure smooth delivery and collaboration.
Participate in Agile ceremonies including daily stand-ups and sprint planning sessions.


What You Need To Have To Be Considered


3+ years of experience in Data Analysis / Data Engineering roles.
Strong expertise in Python and Spark for data processing.
Hands-on experience with SQL for querying and data manipulation.
Experience working with Databricks platform.
Basic understanding of ETL/data pipelines and big data processing concepts.
Experience with version control and collaborative development practices is a plus.
Strong problem-solving and communication skills.

Applications will be accepted until 26 Jun 2026.

The annual salary for this position is between $[62,550 - 84,000] depending on experience and other qualifications of the successful candidate.

Benefits: Cognizant offers the following benefits for this position, subject to applicable eligibility requirements:


Medical/Dental/Vision/Life Insurance
Paid holidays plus Paid Time Off
Long-term/Short-term Disability
Paid Parental Leave

Work in an Agile mindset to accelerate delivery of data platform governance and management

Skills

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role? We'd love to work with you if you have:


5+ years of industry experience along with a completed degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
3+ years of experience on Data Platforms (Data Lake and Data Warehouse), with exposure to various analytics & data project life cycle (e.g. ETL).
3+ years of experience with platform monitoring through tools (Cloudera Manager, Grafana)
2+ years of experience on big data technologies (Cloudera/Hortonworks, Spark, Hive), along with hands-on experience of data manipulation and analysis using tools such as Python or SQL
Able to implement & enhance various dashboards through data visualization tool(s) such as Tableau or PowerBI
Strong communication skills (i.e.: spoken/written/presentation) to engage wide array of users to elicit requirements and feedback
Self-motivated to solve problems and able to work collaboratively with other team members.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong process documentation and optimization skills to articulate workflow requirements
Understanding of data management principles would be an asset
Knowledge of agile project methodology and artifacts, on software/application development lifecycle in an agile fashion would be an asset
Knowledge of relational databases (Oracle, SQL Server, MySQL, PostgreSQL) would be an asset
Productivity/Project collaboration tools experience: Confluence, Jira, Sharepoint
Databricks experience would an asset.
Fluency in Spanish Language to converse confidently for work related communication would be an asset
